% --------------------------------------------------------------
% ARC‑AGI solution using Answer Set Programming
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% board dimensions (rows and columns 0..9)
row(0). row(1). row(2). row(3). row(4). row(5). row(6). row(7). row(8). row(9).
col(0). col(1). col(2). col(3). col(4). col(5). col(6). col(7). col(8). col(9).

output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Identify the unique wall row (full of colour 5)
% --------------------------------------------------------------

row_five_count(R,Count) :- aggregate(N = #count, input(R,C,5), N).

full_wall(R) :- row_five_count(R,10).

% exactly one wall row must be chosen, and it must be a full wall
1 { wall(R) : full_wall(R) } 1.

% the selected wall row really has ten fives
:- wall(R), not full_wall(R).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Colour parity (0 is background, 5 is the wall, others as below)
% --------------------------------------------------------------

even_col(2). even_col(4). even_col(6). even_col(8).
odd_col(1).  odd_col(3).  odd_col(7).  odd_col(9).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Start cells – original non‑zero, non‑5 coloured cells
% --------------------------------------------------------------

start(R,C,Col) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Distance enumerator (0..9)
% --------------------------------------------------------------

dist(0). dist(1). dist(2). dist(3). dist(4). dist(5).
dist(6). dist(7). dist(8). dist(9).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Propagation of colours towards/away the wall
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% even colours – towards the wall
candidate_cell(R,C,Col) :-
    start(R_start,C,Col), even_col(Col), wall(W), R_start < W,
    dist(D),
    R = R_start + D,
    R >= 0,
    R =< 9,
    not input(R,C,5),
    R =< W-1.

candidate_cell(R,C,Col) :-
    start(R_start,C,Col), even_col(Col), wall(W), R_start > W,
    dist(D),
    R = R_start - D,
    R >= 0,
    R =< 9,
    R >= W+1,
    not input(R,C,5).

% odd colours – away from the wall
candidate_cell(R,C,Col) :-
    start(R_start,C,Col), odd_col(Col), wall(W), R_start < W,
    dist(D),
    R = R_start - D,
    R >= 0,
    R =< 9,
    not input(R,C,5).

candidate_cell(R,C,Col) :-
    start(R_start,C,Col), odd_col(Col), wall(W), R_start > W,
    dist(D),
    R = R_start + D,
    R >= 0,
    R =< 9,
    not input(R,C,5).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Justifications for colours
% --------------------------------------------------------------

justif(R,C,Col) :- candidate_cell(R,C,Col).
justif(R,C,5)   :- input(R,C,5).   % wall cells stay 5

justif_nonzero(R,C) :- justif(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Constraints
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% every non‑zero colour must be justified
:- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0, not justif(R,C,Col).

% background 0 cannot be used where any non‑zero colour is justified
:- output(R,C,0), justif_nonzero(R,C).

% wall cells stay 5
:- input(R,C,5), output(R,C,Col), Col != 5.

% original non‑zero cells keep their colour
:- not output(R,C,Col0), input(R,C,Col0), Col0 != 0, Col0 != 5.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Choice – exactly one colour for every cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------

{ output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------

Let's draft final program:

```asp
% board size
row(0). row(1). row(2). row(3). row(4). row(5). row(6). row(7). row(8). row(9).
col(0). col(1). col(2). col(3). col(4). col(5). col(6). col(7). col(8). col(9).

% output cells
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% ----- identify the wall (full row of 5) -----
full_wall(R) :-
    row(R),
    #count { C : col(C), input(R, C, 5) } = 10.

% choose exactly one wall row
1 { wall(R) : full_wall(R) } 1.

% the selected wall must indeed be full
:- wall(R), not full_wall(R).

% parity of colours (excluding 0 and wall colour 5)
even_col(C) :- color(C), C #mod 2 = 0, C != 0, C != 5.
odd_col(C)  :- color(C), C #mod 2 = 1, C != 0, C != 5.

% start cells: original non‑zero, non‑5 cells
start(R0, C, Col) :-
    input(R0, C, Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 5.

% direction of stretching
step_dir(R_start, C, Col, Wall, Step) :-
    wall(Wall),
    even_col(Col), R_start < Wall, Step = 1.
step_dir(R_start, C, Col, Wall, Step) :-
    wall(Wall),
    even_col(Col), R_start > Wall, Step = -1.
step_dir(R_start, C, Col, Wall, Step) :-
    wall(Wall),
    odd_col(Col), R_start < Wall, Step = -1.
step_dir(R_start, C, Col, Wall, Step) :-
    wall(Wall),
    odd_col(Col), R_start > Wall, Step = 1.

% propagation of colour from a start cell
prop(R_start, C, Col, R_cur) :-
    start(R_start, C, Col),
    step_dir(R_start, C, Col, Wall, Step),
    R_cur = R_start + Step,
    R_cur >= 0,
    R_cur <= 9,
    not input(R_cur, C, 5).

prop(R_start, C, Col, R_next) :-
    prop(R_start, C, Col, R_cur),
    step_dir(R_start, C, Col, Wall, Step),
    R_next = R_cur + Step,
    R_next >= 0,
    R_next <= 9,
    not input(R_next, C, 5).

% candidate cells that receive the propagated colour
candidate_cell(R, C, Col) :-
    prop(R_start, C, Col, R),
    R != R_start.

% ----- justifications for output colours -----
justif(R, C, Col) :- input(R, C, Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.
justif(R, C, Col) :- candidate_cell(R, C, Col).
justif(R, C, 5)   :- input(R, C, 5).    % wall cells
justif(R, C, 0)   :- input(R, C, 0).

% ----- constraints -----
% original non‑zero cells keep their colour
:- not output(R, C, Col0), input(R, C, Col0), Col0 != 0, Col0 != 5.

% propagated cells must be coloured
:- candidate_cell(R, C, Col), not output(R, C, Col).

% non‑zero colours must be justified
:- output(R, C, Col), Col != 0, not justif(R, C, Col).

% background 0 cannot appear where a non‑zero colour is justified
nonzero_justif(R, C) :- justif(R, C, Col), Col != 0.
:- output(R, C, 0), nonzero_justif(R, C).

% wall cells stay 5 and cannot be overwritten
:- input(R, C, 5), not output(R, C, 5).
:- output(R, C, Col), input(R, C, 5), Col != 5.

% each cell gets exactly one colour
{ output(R, C, Color) : color(Color), output_cell(R, C) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% ----- end -----

Now define parity and propagation:

even_col(C) :- color(C), C #mod 2 = 0, C != 0, C != 5.

odd_col(C) :- color(C), C #mod 2 = 1, C != 0, C != 5.

start(R0,C,Col) :- input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

step_dir(R_start,C,Col,Wall,Step) :-
    wall(Wall), even_col(Col), R_start < Wall, Step = 1.
step_dir(R_start,C,Col,Wall,Step) :-
    wall(Wall), even_col(Col), R_start > Wall, Step = -1.
step_dir(R_start,C,Col,Wall,Step) :-
    wall(Wall), odd_col(Col), R_start < Wall, Step = -1.
step_dir(R_start,C,Col,Wall,Step) :-
    wall(Wall), odd_col(Col), R_start > Wall, Step = 1.

prop(R_start,C,Col,R_cur) :-
    start(R_start,C,Col),
    step_dir(R_start,C,Col,Wall,Step),
    R_cur = R_start + Step,
    R_cur >= 0,
    R_cur <= 9,
    not input(R_cur,C,5).

prop(R_start,C,Col,R_next) :-
    prop(R_start,C,Col,R_cur),
    step_dir(R_start,C,Col,Wall,Step),
    R_next = R_cur + Step,
    R_next >= 0,
    R_next <= 9,
    not input(R_next,C,5).

candidate_cell(R,C,Col) :-
    prop(R_start,C,Col,R),
    R != R_start.

justif(R,C,Col) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.
justif(R,C,Col) :- candidate_cell(R,C,Col).
justif(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).
justif(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,0).

% Define rows and columns

row(0). row(1). row(2). row(3). row(4). row(5). row(6). row(7). row(8). row(9).

col(0). col(1). col(2). col(3). col(4). col(5). col(6). col(7). col(8). col(9).

output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Identify wall row: row with 5 in every column
missing_five(R,C) :- col(C), not input(R, C, 5).
full_wall(R) :- row(R), not missing_five(R, _).

% Choose exactly one wall row
1 { wall(R) : full_wall(R) } 1.

% The chosen row must be a full wall
:- wall(R), not full_wall(R).

% Parity predicates
even_col(C) :- color(C), C #mod 2 = 0, C != 0, C != 5.
odd_col(C)  :- color(C), C #mod 2 = 1, C != 0, C != 5.

% Start cells
start(R_start, C, Col) :- input(R_start, C, Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

% Direction: step_dir(R_start,C,Col,Wall,Step)
step_dir(R_start, C, Col, Wall, Step) :-
    wall(Wall),
    even_col(Col), R_start < Wall, Step = 1.
step_dir(R_start, C, Col, Wall, Step) :-
    wall(Wall),
    even_col(Col), R_start > Wall, Step = -1.
step_dir(R_start, C, Col, Wall, Step) :-
    wall(Wall),
    odd_col(Col), R_start < Wall, Step = -1.
step_dir(R_start, C, Col, Wall, Step) :-
    wall(Wall),
    odd_col(Col), R_start > Wall, Step = 1.

% Propagation
prop(R_start, C, Col, R_cur) :-
    start(R_start, C, Col),
    step_dir(R_start, C, Col, Wall, Step),
    R_cur = R_start + Step,
    R_cur >= 0,
    R_cur <= 9,
    not input(R_cur, C, 5).

prop(R_start, C, Col, R_next) :-
    prop(R_start, C, Col, R_cur),
    step_dir(R_start, C, Col, Wall, Step),
    R_next = R_cur + Step,
    R_next >= 0,
    R_next <= 9,
    not input(R_next, C, 5).

candidate_cell(R, C, Col) :-
    prop(R_start, C, Col, R),
    R != R_start.

% Justifications
justif(R, C, Col) :- input(R, C, Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.
justif(R, C, Col) :- candidate_cell(R, C, Col).
justif(R, C, 5)   :- input(R, C, 5).
justif(R, C, 0)   :- input(R, C, 0).

% Constraints
% original non-zero cells keep colour
:- not output(R, C, Col0), input(R, C, Col0), Col0 != 0, Col0 != 5.

% propagated cells must be output
:- candidate_cell(R, C, Col), not output(R, C, Col).

% non-zero colours must be justified
:- output(R, C, Col), Col != 0, not justif(R, C, Col).

% background 0 cannot appear where a non-zero colour is justified
nonzero_justif(R, C) :- justif(R, C, Col), Col != 0.
:- output(R, C, 0), nonzero_justif(R, C).

% wall cells stay 5
:- input(R, C, 5), not output(R, C, 5).
:- output(R, C, Col), input(R, C, 5), Col != 5.

% each cell exactly one colour
output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).
{ output(R, C, Color) : color(Color), output_cell(R, C) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% end

Let's assemble step by step with proper syntax:

```
% Board dimensions
row(0). row(1). row(2). row(3). row(4). row(5). row(6). row(7). row(8). row(9).
col(0). col(1). col(2). col(3). col(4). col(5). col(6). col(7). col(8). col(9).

output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Identify rows that contain a 5 in every column
full_wall(R) :-
    row(R),
    #count { C : col(C), input(R, C, 5) } = 10.

% Choose exactly one wall row
1 { wall(R) : full_wall(R) } 1.

% The selected wall must be a full wall
:- wall(R), not full_wall(R).

% Colour parity (exclude 0 and 5)
even_col(C) :- color(C), C #mod 2 = 0, C != 0, C != 5.
odd_col(C)  :- color(C), C #mod 2 = 1, C != 0, C != 5.

% Start cells – original non‑zero, non‑5 cells
start(R_start, C, Col) :-
    input(R_start, C, Col),

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – “stretch‑towards‑or‑away‑from‑the‑5‑wall” rule
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  The hidden transformation can be described as follows
%
%    * The whole board contains a rectangular “wall” made of colour 5.
%      In the training data the wall occupies a single full row
%      (row 6 in the examples).  The row that contains a 5 in
%      *every* column is taken as the centre row C.
%
%    * For every non‑zero, non‑5 cell (R,C,Col) we decide whether
%      we have to stretch the colour towards the wall (if Col is
%      even) or away from it (if Col is odd).
%
%    * The stretching is performed in the same column:
%          – even colour → step towards C but stop *before* the 5‑cell
%          – odd  colour → step away from C until the edge of the
%            board.
%      All cells on the generated line obtain the original colour.
%
%    * The original cell itself keeps its colour.
%
%    * No cell may obtain a colour different from the one forced by
%      the rule – this is expressed by integrity constraints.
%
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Auxiliary predicates
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% all columns that appear in the input
col(C) :- input(_, C, _).

% count how many 5’s are in each row
row_five_count(R,N) :-
    aggregate(N = #count, input(R,_,5), N).

% the (unique) centre row – the row with the maximal number of 5’s
max_five_count(N) :-
    not greater(N,M),
    row_five_count(_,N),
    row_five_count(_,M).

greater(N,M) :- input(R,_,_), R > M.

row_center(C) :-
    row_five_count(C,N),
    max_five_count(M),
    N = M.

% extreme rows of the board
max_row(M) :-
    input(R,_,_),
    M = R,
    not greater(M,R2).

% a cell is above the centre row
above_center(R) :-
    row_center(C),
    R < C.

% a cell is below the centre row
below_center(R) :-
    row_center(C),
    R > C.

% colour is even ?
even(C) :- color(C), (C #mod 2 = 0).

odd(C)  :- color(C), (C #mod 2 = 1).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Domain – all cells that appear in the input
% --------------------------------------------------------------

output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Choice – exactly one colour for every output cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------

{ output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Integrity constraints – enforce the transformation rule
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% 1. every original coloured cell (≠0,≠5) must keep its colour
:- not output(R,C,Col0),
   input(R,C,Col0),
   col(Col0),               % keep the column for readability
   Col0 != 0,
   Col0 != 5,
   not output(R,C,Col0).

% 2. propagation for *even* colours – towards the centre row
%    (stop one row before the first 5‑cell in that column)
:- even(Col0),
   input(R0,C0,Col0),
   above_center(R0),                     % above the wall → go down
   R1 = R0 + 1,
   R1 < row_center(Rc),                  % we are still above the wall
   not input(R1,C0,5),                   % stop before a 5‑cell
   output(R1,C0,Col0).

:- even(Col0),
   input(R0,C0,Col0),
   below_center(R0),                     % below the wall → go up
   R1 = R0 - 1,
   R1 > row_center(Rc),                  % still below the wall
   not input(R1,C0,5),
   output(R1,C0,Col0).

% 3. continuation of the even‑colour stretch
:- even(Col0),
   input(R0,C0,Col0),
   above_center(R0),
   R1 = R0 + 1,
   R1 < row_center(Rc),
   not input(R1,C0,5),
   output(R1,C0,Col0),
   R2 = R1 + 1,
   R2 < row_center(Rc),
   not input(R2,C0,5),
   output(R2,C0,Col0),
   R3 = R2 + 1,
   R3 < row_center(Rc),
   not input(R3,C0,5),
   output(R3,C0,Col0),
   R4 = R3 + 1,
   R4 < row_center(Rc),
   not input(R4,C0,5),
   output(R4,C0,Col0),
   R5 = R4 + 1,
   R5 < row_center(Rc),
   not input(R5,C0,5),
   output(R5,C0,Col0).

:- even(Col0),
   input(R0,C0,Col0),
   below_center(R0),
   R1 = R0 - 1,
   R1 > row_center(Rc),
   not input(R1,C0,5),
   output(R1,C0,Col0),
   R2 = R1 - 1,
   R2 > row_center(Rc),
   not input(R2,C0,5),
   output(R2,C0,Col0),
   R3 = R2 - 1,
   R3 > row_center(Rc),
   not input(R3,C0,5),
   output(R3,C0,Col0),
   R4 = R3 - 1,
   R4 > row_center(Rc),
   not input(R4,C0,5),
   output(R4,C0,Col0).

% 4. propagation for *odd* colours – away from the centre row
%    (stop at the board edge)
:- odd(Col0),
   input(R0,C0,Col0),
   above_center(R0),
   R1 = R0 - 1,
   R1 >= 1,
   not input(R1,C0,5),
   output(R1,C0,Col0).

:- odd(Col0),
   input(R0,C0,Col0),
   below_center(R0),
   R1 = R0 + 1,
   R1 <= max_row(M),
   not input(R1,C0,5),
   output(R1,C0,Col0).

% 5. no other colour may appear in a cell that the rule does not
%    allow.  The constraint simply forbids any assignment that is not
%    produced by the propagation rules above.
:- output(R,C,Col),
   not (   input(R,C,Col)                     % the cell itself has this colour
      or
         (   even(Col),                        % even colour – towards centre
             (   (input(R,C,Col) ;                % original cell
                 (   above_center(R), Col0 = Col, above_center(R), R0 = R+1, R0 < row_center(Rc), not input(R0,C,5), output(R0,C,Col)
                  ;   below_center(R), Col0 = Col, below_center(R), R0 = R-1, R0 > row_center(Rc), not input(R0,C,5), output(R0,C,Col)
                 )
             )
         )
      or
         (   odd(Col),                         % odd colour – away from centre
             (   (input(R,C,Col))               % original cell
                 ;
                 (   above_center(R), R0 = R-1, R0 >= 1, not input(R0,C,5), output(R0,C,Col)
                  ;   below_center(R), R0 = R+1, R0 <= max_row(M), not input(R0,C,5), output(R0,C,Col)
                 )
             )
         )
   ),
   Col != 0,                               % colour 0 is allowed to stay 0
   not ( output(R,C,0) ;                  % 0 is the default background colour
         (   input(R,C,0)                   % and stays 0 only if the input cell is 0
         )
       ).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  End of program
% --------------------------------------------------------------
```

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Parity of colours (colour 5 never participates in stretching)
even_col(0).  even_col(2).  even_col(4).  even_col(6).  even_col(8).
odd_col(1).   odd_col(3).   odd_col(7).   odd_col(9).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. The “wall” row: exactly one row that contains a 5 in every column
is_wall(R) :- R >= 0, R <= 9, 1 { is_wall(R) : R >= 0, R <= 9 } 1.
% the selected row must really be a wall
:- is_wall(R), col(C), not input(R, C, 5).

wall(W) :- is_wall(W).    % convenient name for the wall row

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Helper predicates
block(R, C)          :- input(R, C, 5).               % cannot step onto a 5
other_col(R, C, Col) :-
    input(R, C, Other),
    color(Other),
    Other != 0,
    Other != 5,
    Other != Col.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Direction of stretching (Step = +1 → down, Step = –1 → up)
step_dir(R_start, C, Col, Step, W) :-
    even_col(Col), R_start < W, wall(W), Step = 1.
step_dir(R_start, C, Col, Step, W) :-
    even_col(Col), R_start > W, wall(W), Step = -1.
step_dir(R_start, C, Col, Step, W) :-
    odd_col(Col), R_start < W, wall(W), Step = -1.
step_dir(R_start, C, Col, Step, W) :-
    odd_col(Col), R_start > W, wall(W), Step = 1.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Reachable rows for a colour stretch (including the original cell)
reachable(R_start, C, Col, R_start, R_start, W) :- wall(W).
reachable(R_start, C, Col, R, R0, W) :-
    wall(W),
    step_dir(R_start, C, Col, Step, W),
    R_next = R_start + Step,
    R_next >= 0,
    R_next <= 9,
    not block(R_next, C),
    not other_col(R_next, C, Col),
    reachable(R_next, C, Col, R, R0, W).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Propagated (but not original) cells that obtain the same colour
candidate_output(R, C, Col) :-
    input(R0, C, Col),
    Col != 0, Col != 5,
    reachable(R0, C, Col, R, R0, W),
    R != R0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Constraints fixing the output
% a) Original non‑zero/non‑5 cells must keep their colour
:- not output(R, C, Col0),
   input(R, C, Col0),
   Col0 != 0,
   Col0 != 5.

% b) Wall cells stay 5
:- not output(R, C, 5), is_wall(R).

% c) A colour may appear only if it is justified (original or propagated)
justif(R, C, Col) :- input(R, C, Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.
justif(R, C, Col) :- candidate_output(R, C, Col).
:- output(R, C, Col), Col != 0, not justif(R, C, Col).

% d) Background colour 0 may not be chosen when a non‑zero colour is justified
nonzero_justif(R, C) :- candidate_output(R, C, Col), Col != 0.
:- output(R, C, 0), nonzero_justif(R, C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Choice: each cell receives exactly one colour
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
{ output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------

% ARC-AGI solution using Answer Set Programming

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Domain (10 × 10 board, rows and columns are numbered 0..9)
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
row(0).
row(1).
row(2).
row(3).
row(4).
row(5).
row(6).
row(7).
row(8).
row(9).

col(0).
col(1).
col(2).
col(3).
col(4).
col(5).
col(6).
col(7).
col(8).
col(9).

output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Find the “wall” – the unique row that contains a 5 in every column
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
missing_five(R) :- col(C), not input(R, C, 5).
wall(R)        :- row(R), not missing_five(R).

% exactly one wall row
{ wall(R) : row(R) } = 1.

% the selected row must really be a wall
:- wall(R), missing_five(R).

% any row that is a full wall must be selected as the wall
:- row(R), not missing_five(R), not wall(R).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Parity of colours (only non‑zero, non‑5 colours are relevant)
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
even_col(2). even_col(4). even_col(6). even_col(8).
odd_col(1).  odd_col(3).  odd_col(5).  odd_col(7).  odd_col(9).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Direction of stretching (Step = +1 downwards, -1 upwards)
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
step_dir(R0, C, Col, W, Step) :-
    even_col(Col), R0 < W, wall(W), Step = 1.
step_dir(R0, C, Col, W, Step) :-
    even_col(Col), R0 > W, wall(W), Step = -1.
step_dir(R0, C, Col, W, Step) :-
    odd_col(Col),  R0 < W, wall(W), Step = -1.
step_dir(R0, C, Col, W, Step) :-
    odd_col(Col),  R0 > W, wall(W), Step = 1.

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Start cells – original coloured cells (non‑zero, non‑5)
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
start_cell(R0, C, Col) :-
    input(R0, C, Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 5.

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Propagation from a start cell in the appropriate direction
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
propagate(R_start, C, Col, R_cur, W) :-
    R_cur = R_start,
    start_cell(R_start, C, Col),
    wall(W).

propagate(R_start, C, Col, R_next, W) :-
    propagate(R_start, C, Col, R_cur, W),
    step_dir(R_start, C, Col, W, Step),
    R_next = R_cur + Step,
    R_next >= 0,
    R_next <= 9,
    not input(R_next, C, 5).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Justifications for a colour in a cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
justif(R, C, Col) :- input(R, C, Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.
justif(R, C, Col) :- propagate(R_start, C, Col, R, W), start_cell(R_start, C, Col), R_start != R.
justif(R, C, 5)   :- input(R, C, 5).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Output constraints
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% each cell receives exactly one colour
{ output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% colour must be justified
:- justif(R, C, Col), not output(R, C, Col).

% non‑zero colours may appear only if justified
:- output(R, C, Col), Col != 0, not justif(R, C, Col).

% 5‑cells stay 5 and are never overwritten by another colour
:- input(R, C, 5), not output(R, C, 5).
:- output(R, C, Col), input(R, C, 5), Col != 5.
```

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% ARC‑AGI solution using Answer Set Programming
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% board dimensions (rows and columns 0..9)
row(0). row(1). row(2). row(3). row(4). row(5). row(6). row(7). row(8). row(9).
col(0). col(1). col(2). col(3). col(4). col(5). col(6). col(7). col(8). col(9).

output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Identify the unique wall row (full of colour 5)
% --------------------------------------------------------------

row_five_count(R,Count) :- aggregate(N = #count, input(R,C,5), N).

full_wall(R) :- row_five_count(R,10).

% exactly one wall row must be chosen, and it must be a full wall
1 { wall(R) : full_wall(R) } 1.

% the selected wall row really has ten fives
:- wall(R), not full_wall(R).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Colour parity (0 is background, 5 is the wall, others as below)
% --------------------------------------------------------------

even_col(2). even_col(4). even_col(6). even_col(8).
odd_col(1).  odd_col(3).  odd_col(7).  odd_col(9).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Start cells – original non‑zero, non‑5 coloured cells
% --------------------------------------------------------------

start(R,C,Col) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Distance enumerator (0..9)
% --------------------------------------------------------------

dist(0). dist(1). dist(2). dist(3). dist(4). dist(5).
dist(6). dist(7). dist(8). dist(9).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Propagation of colours towards/away the wall
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% even colours – towards the wall
candidate_cell(R,C,Col) :-
    start(R_start,C,Col), even_col(Col), wall(W), R_start < W,
    dist(D),
    R = R_start + D,
    R >= 0,
    R =< 9,
    not input(R,C,5),
    R =< W-1.

candidate_cell(R,C,Col) :-
    start(R_start,C,Col), even_col(Col), wall(W), R_start > W,
    dist(D),
    R = R_start - D,
    R >= 0,
    R =< 9,
    R >= W+1,
    not input(R,C,5).

% odd colours – away from the wall
candidate_cell(R,C,Col) :-
    start(R_start,C,Col), odd_col(Col), wall(W), R_start < W,
    dist(D),
    R = R_start - D,
    R >= 0,
    R =< 9,
    not input(R,C,5).

candidate_cell(R,C,Col) :-
    start(R_start,C,Col), odd_col(Col), wall(W), R_start > W,
    dist(D),
    R = R_start + D,
    R >= 0,
    R =< 9,
    not input(R,C,5).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Justifications for colours
% --------------------------------------------------------------

justif(R,C,Col) :- candidate_cell(R,C,Col).
justif(R,C,5)   :- input(R,C,5).   % wall cells stay 5

justif_nonzero(R,C) :- justif(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Constraints
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% every non‑zero colour must be justified
:- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0, not justif(R,C,Col).

% background 0 cannot be used where any non‑zero colour is justified
:- output(R,C,0), justif_nonzero(R,C).

% wall cells stay 5
:- input(R,C,5), output(R,C,Col), Col != 5.

% original non‑zero cells keep their colour
:- not output(R,C,Col0), input(R,C,Col0), Col0 != 0, Col0 != 5.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Choice – exactly one colour for every cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------

{ output(R,C,Color) : color(Color), output_cell(R,C) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% ARC‑AGI – deterministic propagation towards/away from the wall
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% board size (fixed 10×10)
row(0). row(1). row(2). row(3). row(4). row(5). row(6). row(7). row(8). row(9).
col(0). col(1). col(2). col(3). col(4). col(5). col(6). col(7). col(8). col(9).

output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Identify the unique wall row (full of colour 5)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
full_wall(R) :-
    row(R),
    #count { C : col(C), input(R, C, 5) } = 10.

% exactly one wall row must be selected
1 { wall(R) : full_wall(R) } 1.

% the selected row must really be a full wall
:- wall(R), not full_wall(R).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Parity of colours (colour 5 is the wall, colour 0 is background)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
even_col(C) :- color(C), C #mod 2 = 0, C != 0, C != 5.
odd_col(C)  :- color(C), C #mod 2 = 1, C != 0, C != 5.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Start cells – original non‑zero, non‑5 cells
% --------------------------------------------------------------
start(R_start, C, Col) :-
    input(R_start, C, Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 5.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Direction of stretching (Step = +1 down, -1 up)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
step_dir(R_start, C, Col, W, Step) :-
    wall(W),
    even_col(Col), R_start < W, Step = 1.
step_dir(R_start, C, Col, W, Step) :-
    wall(W),
    even_col(Col), R_start > W, Step = -1.
step_dir(R_start, C, Col, W, Step) :-
    wall(W),
    odd_col(Col), R_start < W, Step = -1.
step_dir(R_start, C, Col, W, Step) :-
    wall(W),
    odd_col(Col), R_start > W, Step = 1.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Propagation of colour from a start cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------
prop(R_start, C, Col, R_cur) :-
    start(R_start, C, Col),
    step_dir(R_start, C, Col, W, Step),
    R_cur = R_start + Step,
    R_cur >= 0,
    R_cur <= 9,
    not input(R_cur, C, 5).

prop(R_start, C, Col, R_next) :-
    prop(R_start, C, Col, R_cur),
    step_dir(R_start, C, Col, W, Step),
    R_next = R_cur + Step,
    R_next >= 0,
    R_next <= 9,
    not input(R_next, C, 5).

% candidate cells that receive the propagated colour (excluding the start cell)
candidate_cell(R, C, Col) :-
    prop(R_start, C, Col, R),
    R != R_start.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Justifications for a colour in a cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------
justif(R, C, Col) :- input(R, C, Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.
justif(R, C, Col) :- candidate_cell(R, C, Col).
justif(R, C, 5)   :- input(R, C, 5).   % wall cells stay 5
justif(R, C, 0)   :- input(R, C, 0).   % background

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Constraints enforcing the transformation
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% original non‑zero cells keep their colour
:- not output(R, C, Col0), input(R, C, Col0), Col0 != 0, Col0 != 5.

% propagated cells must appear in the output
:- candidate_cell(R, C, Col), not output(R, C, Col).

% non‑zero colours may appear only if justified
:- output(R, C, Col), Col != 0, not justif(R, C, Col).

% background colour 0 cannot be used where a non‑zero colour is justified
nonzero_justif(R, C) :- justif(R, C, Col), Col != 0.
:- output(R, C, 0), nonzero_justif(R, C).

% wall cells stay 5 and cannot be overwritten
:- input(R, C, 5), not output(R, C, 5).
:- output(R, C, Col), input(R, C, 5), Col != 5.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Exactly one colour for every cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------
{ output(R, C, Color) : color(Color), output_cell(R, C) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------

refinements.thinking:

Actually clingo supports #mod in aggregations or in arithmetic? In earlier versions, #mod is an operator for integer division remainder. But perhaps the syntax should be "C #mod 2 = 0"? That may be okay. However errors were lexer error unexpected #mod. Might be because need parentheses: "C #mod 2 = 0" maybe okay. But the parser might need parentheses around the modulo expression? Usually you write "(C #mod 2) = 0". So we need to fix.

Also unsafe variables errors due to using variables not grounded; we need to ensure that all variables are bounded. In ASP, we can use "foreach" or "ground" style, but easier is to avoid dynamic variables inside rules. We can predefine row/col, and use #int etc.

Simplify: We can solve by directly replicating the transformation: The wall row is a row where all cells are 5. In training examples, wall row is row 6 (index 6). We can identify wall row as the row that has a 5 in every column (i.e., count of 5 in that row is 10). Use #count aggregator with ground.

We need to produce exactly the expected output. We can define output cells based on transformation rules: For each non-zero non-5 cell, we stretch its colour either towards the wall (if colour even) or away (if colour odd). The stretching continues in same column until hitting a 5 cell or board edge.

Full ASP program:

```
% Board dimensions
row(0). row(1). row(2). row(3). row(4). row(5). row(6). row(7). row(8). row(9).
col(0). col(1). col(2). col(3). col(4). col(5). col(6). col(7). col(8). col(9).

% Cell exists if present in input
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ----- Identify the wall row (full of 5) -----
% Exactly one wall row is chosen
{ wall(R) : row(R) } 1.

% The chosen row must really consist of ten 5's
:- wall(R), col(C), not input(R, C, 5).

% ----- Colour parity (colour 5 is wall, colour 0 is background) -----
even_col(2). even_col(4). even_col(6). even_col(8).
odd_col(1).  odd_col(3).  odd_col(7).  odd_col(9).

% ----- Original coloured cells that are not 0 or 5 -----
start(R0,C,Col) :-
    input(R0, C, Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 5.

% ----- Direction of stretching (Step = +1 down, -1 up) -----
direction(R0,C,Col,Step,W) :-
    start(R0,C,Col), even_col(Col), wall(W), R0 < W, Step = 1.
direction(R0,C,Col,Step,W) :-
    start(R0,C,Col), even_col(Col), wall(W), R0 > W, Step = -1.
direction(R0,C,Col,Step,W) :-
    start(R0,C,Col), odd_col(Col), wall(W), R0 < W, Step = -1.
direction(R0,C,Col,Step,W) :-
    start(R0,C,Col), odd_col(Col), wall(W), R0 > W, Step = 1.

% ----- Propagation of a colour from a start cell -----
propagate(R0,C,Col,R0,W) :-
    start(R0,C,Col), direction(R0,C,Col,Step,W).
propagate(R0,C,Col,Rnext,W) :-
    propagate(R0,C,Col,Rcur,W),
    direction(R0,C,Col,Step,W),
    Rnext = Rcur + Step,
    Rnext >= 0,
    Rnext <= 9,
    not input(Rnext, C, 5).

% ----- Derived output for propagated cells (non‑zero, non‑5) -----
output(R,C,Col) :-
    start(R0,C,Col),
    propagate(R0,C,Col,R,W),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 5.

% ----- Wall cells stay 5 -----
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% ----- Background colour 0 for cells without any derived output -----
has_output(R,C) :- output(R,C,_).
output(R,C,0) :- output_cell(R,C), not has_output(R,C).

% ----- Exactly one colour per cell (guess) -----
{ output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ----- Original coloured cells must keep their colour -----
:- not output(R,C,Col0),
   input(R,C,Col0),
   Col0 != 0,
   Col0 != 5.

% ----- Wall cells must stay 5 -----
:- input(R,C,5), not output(R,C,5).

% No other non‑zero colour may appear unless justified (optional, can be omitted)
% We skip this constraint because derived output already guarantees justification.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% End of program
% --------------------------------------------------------------
```

Let's craft final program as per the analysis, ensure proper syntax.

Make sure we use "col(0). col(1)." etc. row/col definitions.

Define:

row(0). row(1). ... row(9).
col(0). col(1). ... col(9).

Define output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

Define wall(R) guess and constraint:

{ wall(R) : row(R) } 1.

:- wall(R), col(C), not input(R,C,5).

Define even_col/1 and odd_col/1:

even_col(2). even_col(4). even_col(6). even_col(8).
odd_col(1). odd_col(3). odd_col(7). odd_col(9).

Define start:

start(R0,C,Col) :- input(R0,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

Define direction:

direction(R0,C,Col,Step,W) :-
    start(R0,C,Col), even_col(Col), wall(W), R0 < W, Step = 1.
direction(R0,C,Col,Step,W) :-
    start(R0,C,Col), even_col(Col), wall(W), R0 > W, Step = -1.
direction(R0,C,Col,Step,W) :-
    start(R0,C,Col), odd_col(Col), wall(W), R0 < W, Step = -1.
direction(R0,C,Col,Step,W) :-
    start(R0,C,Col), odd_col(Col), wall(W), R0 > W, Step = 1.

Define propagate:

propagate(R0,C,Col,Rcur,W) :-
    start(R0,C,Col), direction(R0,C,Col,Step,W), Rcur = R0.
propagate(R0,C,Col,Rnext,W) :-
    propagate(R0,C,Col,Rcur,W),
    direction(R0,C,Col,Step,W),
    Rnext = Rcur + Step,
    Rnext >= 0,
    Rnext <= 9,
    not input(Rnext, C, 5).

Define derived output for non-zero:

% Board size (rows and columns 0..9)
row(0). row(1). row(2). row(3). row(4). row(5). row(6). row(7). row(8). row(9).
col(0). col(1). col(2). col(3). col(4). col(5). col(6). col(7). col(8). col(9).

% Cell for which we must decide the output colour
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Identify the wall row (full of colour 5)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one row is chosen as the wall
{ wall(R) : row(R) } 1.

% The chosen row must really contain ten 5's
:- wall(R), col(C), not input(R, C, 5).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Colour parity (colour 5 is the wall, colour 0 is background)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
even_col(2). even_col(4). even_col(6). even_col(8).
odd_col(1).  odd_col(3).  odd_col(7).  odd_col(9).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Original coloured cells (non‑zero, non‑5) are the sources of stretching
% --------------------------------------------------------------
start(R0,C,Col) :-
    input(R0, C, Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 5.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Direction of stretching (Step = +1 downwards, -1 upwards)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
direction(R0,C,Col,Step,W) :-
    start(R0,C,Col), even_col(Col), wall(W), R0 < W, Step = 1.
direction(R0,C,Col,Step,W) :-
    start(R0,C,Col), even_col(Col), wall(W), R0 > W, Step = -1.
direction(R0,C,Col,Step,W) :-
    start(R0,C,Col), odd_col(Col), wall(W), R0 < W, Step = -1.
direction(R0,C,Col,Step,W) :-
    start(R0,C,Col), odd_col(Col), wall(W), R0 > W, Step = 1.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Propagation of a colour from a start cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------
propagate(R0,C,Col,Rcur,W) :-
    start(R0,C,Col), direction(R0,C,Col,Step,W), Rcur = R0.
propagate(R0,C,Col,Rnext,W) :-
    propagate(R0,C,Col,Rcur,W),
    direction(R0,C,Col,Step,W),
    Rnext = Rcur + Step,
    Rnext >= 0,
    Rnext <= 9,
    not input(Rnext, C, 5).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Output for cells reached by a colour stretch (non‑zero, non‑5)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Col) :-
    start(R0,C,Col),
    propagate(R0,C,Col,R,W),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 5.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Wall cells stay colour 5
% --------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Background colour 0 for cells that have no derived output
% --------------------------------------------------------------
has_output(R,C) :- output(R,C,_).

output(R,C,0) :- output_cell(R,C), not has_output(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one colour for each cell (guess)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
{ output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Constraints
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Original non‑zero cells must keep their colour
:- not output(R,C,Col0),
   input(R,C,Col0),
   Col0 != 0,
   Col0 != 5.

% Wall cells must stay 5
:- input(R,C,5), not output(R,C,5).

% (Optional) No other non‑zero colour may appear without justification
% (justified by construction, so this constraint is not needed).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% End of program
% --------------------------------------------------------------
```

cell_exists(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

    input(R1,C1,Color), Color != 0.

    C1 >= 1, C2 >= C1, C2 <= MAX,
    input(R1,C1,0).

```asp
#const MAX = 30.                     % grid size – large enough for all examples

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Auxiliary predicates
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
cell_exists(R,C)   :- input(R,C,_).          % a cell that actually appears in the input
output_cell(R,C)   :- cell_exists(R,C).      % cells that may have an output value

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Guess a solid rectangle of a non‑zero colour (the “pattern”)
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1, R2 >= R1, R2 <= MAX,
    C1 >= 1, C2 >= C1, C2 <= MAX,
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Guess a zero rectangle (the area that will be filled with the pattern)
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
rect_z(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1, R2 >= R1, R2 <= MAX,
    C1 >= 1, C2 >= C1, C2 <= MAX,
    input(R1,C1,0).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% All cells of a guessed pattern rectangle must really have that colour
% and must be inside the given input grid
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
:- rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not cell_exists(R,C).

:- rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Col2), Col2 != Col.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% All cells of a guessed zero rectangle must really be zero and inside
% the input grid
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
:- rect_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not cell_exists(R,C).

:- rect_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% The pattern rectangle must be at least 2 × 2
:- rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), R2 = R1, C2 = C1.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Copy the colour of a solid rectangle onto every zero rectangle of the
% same size (if the dimensions match).  Only cells that belong to a
% zero rectangle whose size equals the size of a solid rectangle obtain
% the colour of that solid rectangle.
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
    rect_z(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
    R >= ZR1, R <= ZR2,
    C >= ZC1, C <= ZC2,
    (R2 - R1) = (ZR2 - ZR1),                % same height
    (C2 - C1) = (ZC2 - ZC1).                % same width

% At least one cell has to be coloured by the copy operation
:- not copy_target(R,C,_).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Output handling
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
{ output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).   % exactly one colour per cell

% Preserve every originally coloured cell
:- output(R,C,Out), input(R,C,In), In != 0, Out != In.

% If a cell is covered by the copy operation it must receive the copied colour
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% Zero cells that are not covered by the copy operation must stay zero
:- output(R,C,Out), input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), Out != 0.

% (optional safety – copy cannot overwrite a non‑zero cell)
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

full_program:
#const MAX = 30.                     % grid size – large enough for all examples

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Auxiliary predicates
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
cell_exists(R,C)   :- input(R,C,_).          % a cell that actually appears in the input
output_cell(R,C)   :- cell_exists(R,C).      % cells that may have an output value

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Guess a solid rectangle of a non‑zero colour (the “pattern”)
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1, R2 >= R1, R2 <= MAX,
    C1 >= 1, C2 >= C1, C2 <= MAX,
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Guess a zero rectangle (the area that will be filled with the pattern)
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
rect_z(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1, R2 >= R1, R2 <= MAX,
    C1 >= 1, C2 >= C1, C2 <= MAX,
    input(R1,C1,0).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% All cells of a guessed pattern rectangle must really have that colour
% and must be inside the given input grid
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
:- rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not cell_exists(R,C).

:- rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Col2), Col2 != Col.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% All cells of a guessed zero rectangle must really be zero and inside
% the input grid
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
:- rect_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not cell_exists(R,C).

:- rect_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% The pattern rectangle must be at least 2 × 2
:- rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), R2 = R1, C2 = C1.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Copy the colour of a solid rectangle onto every zero rectangle of the
% same size (if the dimensions match).  Only cells that belong to a
% zero rectangle whose size equals the size of a solid rectangle obtain
% the colour of that solid rectangle.
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
    rect_z(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
    R >= ZR1, R <= ZR2,
    C >= ZC1, C <= ZC2,
    (R2 - R1) = (ZR2 - ZR1),                % same height
    (C2 - C1) = (ZC2 - ZC1).                % same width

% At least one cell has to be coloured by the copy operation
:- not copy_target(R,C,_).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Output handling
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
{ output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).   % exactly one colour per cell

% Preserve every originally coloured cell
:- output(R,C,Out), input(R,C,In), In != 0, Out != In.

% If a cell is covered by the copy operation it must receive the copied colour
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% Zero cells that are not covered by the copy operation must stay zero
:- output(R,C,Out), input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), Out != 0.

% (optional safety – copy cannot overwrite a non‑zero cell)
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

#const MAX = 30.

% pattern rectangle (solid colour)
pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% all cells of a pattern rectangle have that colour
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,Col).

:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col.

% zero rectangle (area where pattern will be copied)
zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,0).

copy_rect(pr1,pr2,pc1,pc2,zr1,zr2,zc1,zc2,Col) :-
    pattern(pr1,pr2,pc1,pc2,Col),
    zero_z(zr1,zr2,zc1,zc2),
    (pr2 - pr1) =:= (zr2 - zr1),
    (pc2 - pc1) =:= (zc2 - zc1).

% cells that receive the copied colour
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-

    R >= zr1, R <= zr2,
    C >= zc1, C <= zc2,
    Dr = R - zr1,
    Dc = C - zc1,
    Rp = pr1 + Dr,
    Cp = pc1 + Dc,
    input(Rp,Cp,Col).

% all cells that appear in the input
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% exactly one colour per cell (including zero)
{output(R,C,Col) : color(Col)} 1 :- cell(R,C).

% preserve originally coloured cells
:- input(R,C,In), In != 0, output(R,C,Out), Out != In.

% copy operation must be respected
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% zero cells not covered by copy must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% at least one copy must exist
:- not copy_target(R,C,_).

% copy must not overwrite a non‑zero cell (safety)
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0.

```asp
#const MAX = 30.

% --- guess a solid rectangle of a non‑zero colour (the pattern) ---
pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% --- enforce that every cell of the pattern rectangle really has colour Col ---
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,Col).

% --- the pattern rectangle must not contain a different non‑zero colour ---
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col.

% --- guess a zero rectangle (the area where the pattern will be copied) ---
zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% --- zero rectangle must consist only of zeros ---
:- zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,0).

% --- rectangles that match in size can be used for copying ---
copy_rect(pr1,pr2,pc1,pc2,zr1,zr2,zc1,zc2,Col) :-
    pattern(pr1,pr2,pc1,pc2,Col),
    zero_z(zr1,zr2,zc1,zc2),
    (pr2 - pr1) =:= (zr2 - zr1),
    (pc2 - pc1) =:= (zc2 - zc1).

% --- cells that receive the copied colour ---
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    copy_rect(pr1,pr2,pc1,pc2,zr1,zr2,zc1,zc2,Col),
    R >= zr1, R <= zr2,
    C >= zc1, C <= zc2,
    Dr = R - zr1,
    Dc = C - zc1,
    Rp = pr1 + Dr,
    Cp = pc1 + Dc,
    input(Rp,Cp,Col).

% --- all cells that appear in the input ---
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% --- exactly one colour per cell (including zero) ---
{output(R,C,Col) : color(Col)} 1 :- cell(R,C).

% --- preserve originally coloured cells ---
:- input(R,C,In), In != 0, output(R,C,Out), Out != In.

% --- copy must be respected ---
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% --- zero cells not covered by the copy must stay zero ---
:- input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% --- there must be at least one copy operation ---
:- not copy_target(R,C,_).

% --- copy must not overwrite a non‑zero cell (safety) ---
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0.
```

refinements.response:
```asp
#const MAX = 30.

% pattern rectangle (solid colour)
pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% all cells of the pattern rectangle really have colour Col
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,Col).

% the pattern rectangle must not contain a different non‑zero colour
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col.

% zero rectangle (area where the pattern will be copied)
zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% zero rectangle must consist only of zeros
:- zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,0).

% matching rectangles for copying
copy_rect(pr1,pr2,pc1,pc2,zr1,zr2,zc1,zc2,Col) :-
    pattern(pr1,pr2,pc1,pc2,Col),
    zero_z(zr1,zr2,zc1,zc2),
    (pr2 - pr1) =:= (zr2 - zr1),
    (pc2 - pc1) =:= (zc2 - zc1).

% cells that receive the copied colour
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    copy_rect(pr1,pr2,pc1,pc2,zr1,zr2,zc1,zc2,Col),
    R >= zr1, R <= zr2,
    C >= zc1, C <= zc2,
    Dr = R - zr1,
    Dc = C - zc1,
    Rp = pr1 + Dr,
    Cp = pc1 + Dc,
    input(Rp,Cp,Col).

% all cells that appear in the input
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% exactly one colour per cell (including zero)
{output(R,C,Col) : color(Col)} 1 :- cell(R,C).

% preserve originally coloured cells
:- input(R,C,In), In != 0, output(R,C,Out), Out != In.

% copy must be respected
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% zero cells not covered by the copy must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% there must be at least one copy operation
:- not copy_target(R,C,_).

% copy must not overwrite a non‑zero cell (safety)
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0.
```

:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),

   not input(R,C,Col).

% the pattern rectangle must not contain a different non‑zero colour
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col.

% zero rectangle (area where the pattern will be copied)
zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% zero rectangle must consist only of zeros
:- zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,0).

% matching rectangles for copying
copy_rect(pr1,pr2,pc1,pc2,zr1,zr2,zc1,zc2,Col) :-
    pattern(pr1,pr2,pc1,pc2,Col),
    zero_z(zr1,zr2,zc1,zc2),
    (pr2 - pr1) =:= (zr2 - zr1),
    (pc2 - pc1) =:= (zc2 - zc1).

% cells that receive the copied colour
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    copy_rect(pr1,pr2,pc1,pc2,zr1,zr2,zc1,zc2,Col),
    R >= zr1, R <= zr2,
    C >= zc1, C <= zc2,
    Dr = R - zr1,
    Dc = C - zc1,
    Rp = pr1 + Dr,
    Cp = pc1 + Dc,
    input(Rp,Cp,Col).

% all cells that appear in the input
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% exactly one colour per cell (including zero)
{output(R,C,Col) : color(Col)} 1 :- cell(R,C).

% preserve originally coloured cells
:- input(R,C,In), In != 0, output(R,C,Out), Out != In.

% copy must be respected
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% zero cells not covered by the copy must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% there must be at least one copy operation

#const MAX = 30.

% cell predicate for all cells in the input grid
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% exactly one colour per cell (including zero)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% preserve originally coloured cells
:- input(R,C,In), In != 0, output(R,C,Out), Out != In.

% ---------- guess copy operations ----------
% copy(R1,R2,C1,C2,Rz1,Rz2,Cz1,Cz2,Col)
%   source rectangle (R1..R2, C1..C2) of colour Col
%   dest rectangle   (Rz1..Rz2, Cz1..Cz2) of zeros, same size
copy(R1,R2,C1,C2,Rz1,Rz2,Cz1,Cz2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    Rz2 >= Rz1, Cz2 >= Cz1,
    Rz2 <= MAX, Cz2 <= MAX,
    % dimensions match
    (R2 - R1) #= (Rz2 - Rz1),
    (C2 - C1) #= (Cz2 - Cz1),
    % at least 2x2
    (R2 - R1) #>= 1,
    (C2 - C1) #>= 1,
    % source rectangle must consist only of Col (no other colour, no zero)
    R >= R1, R <= R2,
    C >= C1, C <= C2,
    input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col,
    input(R,C,0).               % forbid zeros
    :- false.   % dummy to enforce all constraints? Actually need separate integrity rules, not here.

% Zero rectangle must consist only of zeros
:- copy(R1,R2,C1,C2,Rz1,Rz2,Cz1,Cz2,Col),
   R >= Rz1, R <= Rz2,
   C >= Cz1, C <= Cz2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0.

% source rectangle cells must be non‑zero (already ensured by earlier rule but we also need to forbid zeros)
:- copy(R1,R2,C1,C2,Rz1,Rz2,Cz1,Cz2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,0).

% ---------- copy target cells ----------
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    copy(R1,R2,C1,C2,Rz1,Rz2,Cz1,Cz2,Col),
    R #>= Rz1, R #=< Rz2,
    C #>= Cz1, C #=< Cz2,
    SR #= R1 + (R - Rz1),
    SC #= C1 + (C - Cz1),
    SR #=< R2, SC #=< C2,
    input(SR,SC,Col).

% If a cell is covered by a copy, it must receive the copied colour
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% Zero cells not covered must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% There must be at least one copy operation
:- not copy(R1,R2,C1,C2,Rz1,Rz2,Cz1,Cz2,Col).

% ---------- helper: ensure source rectangle really has the colour ----------
% (already enforced above but we need integrity rules)
:- copy(R1,R2,C1,C2,Rz1,Rz2,Cz1,Cz2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col.

% (forbid zeros inside source rectangle)
:- copy(R1,R2,C1,C2,Rz1,Rz2,Cz1,Cz2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,0).

% Output diagram (for debugging) – not needed, just generate output atoms.
```

#const MAX = 30.

cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% exactly one colour per cell
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% preserve original non-zero cells
:- input(R,C,In), In != 0, output(R,C,Out), Out != In.

% ----- guess a solid rectangle of a non‑zero colour (pattern) -----
pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% ----- guess a zero rectangle (destination) -----
zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% ----- source rectangle must be solid -----
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col.

% ----- dest rectangle must consist only of zeros -----
:- zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0.

% ----- matching rectangles for copying -----
copy_rect(P1,P2,Q1,Q2,Z1,Z2,W1,W2,Col) :-
    pattern(P1,P2,Q1,Q2,Col),
    zero_z(Z1,Z2,W1,W2),
    (P2 - P1) #= (Z2 - Z1),
    (Q2 - Q1) #= (W2 - W1).

% cells that receive the copied colour
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    copy_rect(pr1,pr2,pc1,pc2,zr1,zr2,zc1,zc2,Col),
    R #>= zr1, R #<= zr2,
    C #>= zc1, C #<= zc2,
    SR #= pr1 + (R - zr1),
    SC #= pc1 + (C - zc1),
    SR #<= pr2, SC #<= pc2,
    input(SR,SC,Col).

% at least one copy operation
:- not copy_target(R,C,_).

% copy must be respected
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% zero cells not covered must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% copy cannot overwrite a non‑zero cell (safety)
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0.

% output handling (cardinality)
```

```
#const MAX = 30.

% all cells of the input grid
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% exactly one colour per cell (including zero)
#count {output(R,C,Col) : color(Col)} = 1 :- cell(R,C).

% preserve originally coloured cells
:- input(R,C,In), In != 0, output(R,C,Out), Out != In.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Guess a solid rectangle of a non‑zero colour (pattern)
% -------------------------------------------------
pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Guess a zero rectangle (destination)
% -------------------------------------------------
zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% source rectangle must contain only the pattern colour
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col.

% source rectangle must contain no zero cell
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,0).

% destination rectangle must contain only zeros
:- zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Matching rectangles for copying
% -------------------------------------------------
copy_rect(P1,P2,Q1,Q2,Z1,Z2,W1,W2,Col) :-
    pattern(P1,P2,Q1,Q2,Col),
    zero_z(Z1,Z2,W1,W2),
    (P2 - P1) #= (Z2 - Z1),
    (Q2 - Q1) #= (W2 - W1).

% cells that receive the copied colour
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    copy_rect(pr1,pr2,pc1,pc2,zr1,zr2,zc1,zc2,Col),
    R #>= zr1, R #<= zr2,
    C #>= zc1, C #<= zc2,
    SR #= pr1 + (R - zr1),
    SC #= pc1 + (C - zc1),
    SR #<= pr2, SC #<= pc2,
    input(SR,SC,Col).

% at least one copy operation
:- not copy_target(R,C,_).

% copy must be respected
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% zero cells not covered must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% copy cannot overwrite a non‑zero cell (safety)
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0.
```

#const MAX = 30.

% cells that appear in the input
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% exactly one colour per cell (including zero)
#count {output(R,C,Col) : color(Col)} = 1 :- cell(R,C).

% preserve originally coloured cells
:- input(R,C,In), In != 0, output(R,C,Out), Out != In.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Guess a solid rectangle of a non‑zero colour (pattern)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Guess a zero rectangle (destination)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% source rectangle must contain only the pattern colour and no zeros
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col.
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,0).

% destination rectangle must contain only zeros
:- zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one pattern rectangle and exactly one zero rectangle
% --------------------------------------------------------------
#count {pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col)} = 1 :- true.
#count {zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2)} = 1 :- true.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Matching rectangles for copying (pattern → zero)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
copy_rect(P1,P2,Q1,Q2,Z1,Z2,WC1,WC2,Col) :-
    pattern(P1,P2,Q1,Q2,Col),
    zero_z(Z1,Z2,WC1,WC2),
    (P2 - P1) #= (Z2 - Z1),
    (Q2 - Q1) #= (WC2 - WC1).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Cells that receive the copied colour
% --------------------------------------------------------------
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    copy_rect(pr1,pr2,pc1,pc2,zr1,zr2,wc1,wc2,Col),
    R #>= zr1, R #<= zr2,
    C #>= wc1, C #<= wc2,
    Dr #= R - zr1,
    Dc #= C - wc1,
    SR #= pr1 + Dr,
    SC #= pc1 + Dc,
    SR #<= pr2, SC #<= pc2,
    input(SR,SC,Col).

% at least one copy operation
#count {copy_target(R,C,Col) : true} >= 1 :- true.

% copy must be respected
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% zero cells not covered must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% copy cannot overwrite a non‑zero cell (safety)
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0.
```

#const MAX = 30.

% all cells that appear in the input
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% exactly one colour per cell (including zero)
#count {output(R,C,Col) : color(Col)} = 1 :- cell(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Guess a zero rectangle (destination)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% source rectangle must contain only the pattern colour (no other colour, no zero)
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col.
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,0).

% destination rectangle must contain only zeros
:- zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0.

% exactly one pattern rectangle and exactly one zero rectangle
#count {pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) : true} = 1 :- true.
#count {zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2) : true} = 1 :- true.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Matching rectangles for copying (pattern -> zero)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
copy_rect(P1,P2,C1,C2,Z1,Z2,ZC1,ZC2,Col) :-
    pattern(P1,P2,C1,C2,Col),

% zero cells not covered must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% copy cannot overwrite a non‑zero cell (safety)
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0.

% all cells that appear in the input
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% exactly one colour per cell (including zero)
#count {output(R,C,Col) : color(Col)} = 1 :- cell(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
copy_rect(Pr1,Pr2,Pc1,Pc2,Zr1,Zr2,Zc1,Zc2,Col) :-

% zero cells not covered must stay zero

```asp
#const MAX = 30.                     % grid size – large enough for all examples

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Auxiliary predicates
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
cell_exists(R,C)   :- input(R,C,_).          % a cell that actually appears in the input
output_cell(R,C)   :- cell_exists(R,C).      % cells that may have an output value

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Guess a solid rectangle of a non‑zero colour (the “pattern”)
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1, R2 >= R1, R2 <= MAX,
    C1 >= 1, C2 >= C1, C2 <= MAX,
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Guess a zero rectangle (the area that will be filled with the pattern)
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
rect_z(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1, R2 >= R1, R2 <= MAX,
    C1 >= 1, C2 >= C1, C2 <= MAX,
    input(R1,C1,0).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% All cells of a guessed pattern rectangle must really have that colour
% and must be inside the given input grid
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
:- rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not cell_exists(R,C).

:- rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Col2), Col2 != Col.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% All cells of a guessed zero rectangle must really be zero and inside
% the input grid
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
:- rect_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not cell_exists(R,C).

:- rect_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% The pattern rectangle must be at least 2 × 2
:- rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), R2 = R1, C2 = C1.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Copy the colour of a solid rectangle onto every zero rectangle of the
% same size (if the dimensions match).  Only cells that belong to a
% zero rectangle whose size equals the size of a solid rectangle obtain
% the colour of that solid rectangle.
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
    rect_z(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
    R >= ZR1, R <= ZR2,
    C >= ZC1, C <= ZC2,
    (R2 - R1) = (ZR2 - ZR1),                % same height
    (C2 - C1) = (ZC2 - ZC1).                % same width

% At least one cell has to be coloured by the copy operation
:- not copy_target(R,C,_).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Output handling
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
{ output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).   % exactly one colour per cell

% Preserve every originally coloured cell
:- output(R,C,Out), input(R,C,In), In != 0, Out != In.

% If a cell is covered by the copy operation it must receive the copied colour
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% Zero cells that are not covered by the copy operation must stay zero
:- output(R,C,Out), input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), Out != 0.

% (optional safety – copy cannot overwrite a non‑zero cell)
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

#const MAX = 30.

% pattern rectangle (solid colour)
pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% all cells of the pattern rectangle really have colour Col
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,Col).

% the pattern rectangle must not contain a different non‑zero colour
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col.

% zero rectangle (area where the pattern will be copied)
zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% zero rectangle must consist only of zeros
:- zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,0).

% matching rectangles for copying
copy_rect(pr1,pr2,pc1,pc2,zr1,zr2,zc1,zc2,Col) :-
    pattern(pr1,pr2,pc1,pc2,Col),
    zero_z(zr1,zr2,zc1,zc2),
    (pr2 - pr1) =:= (zr2 - zr1),
    (pc2 - pc1) =:= (zc2 - zc1).

% cells that receive the copied colour
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    copy_rect(pr1,pr2,pc1,pc2,zr1,zr2,zc1,zc2,Col),
    R >= zr1, R <= zr2,
    C >= zc1, C <= zc2,
    Dr = R - zr1,
    Dc = C - zc1,
    Rp = pr1 + Dr,
    Cp = pc1 + Dc,
    input(Rp,Cp,Col).

% all cells that appear in the input
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% exactly one colour per cell (including zero)
{output(R,C,Col) : color(Col)} 1 :- cell(R,C).

% preserve originally coloured cells
:- input(R,C,In), In != 0, output(R,C,Out), Out != In.

% copy must be respected
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% zero cells not covered by the copy must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% there must be at least one copy operation
:- not copy_target(R,C,_).

% copy must not overwrite a non‑zero cell (safety)
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0.

% cells that receive the copied colour
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    copy_rect(Pr1,Pr2,Pc1,Pc2,Zr1,Zr2,Zc1,Zc2,Col),
    R #>= Zr1, R #<= Zr2,
    C #>= Zc1, C #<= Zc2,
    Dr #= R - Zr1,
    Dc #= C - Zc1,
    SR #= Pr1 + Dr,
    SC #= Pc1 + Dc,
    SR #<= Pr2, SC #<= Pc2,
    input(SR,SC,Col).

% at least one copy operation
#count {copy_target(R,C,Col) : true} >= 1 :- true.

% copy must be respected
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% zero cells not covered must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% copy cannot overwrite a non‑zero cell (safety)
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0.

```
% Define the domain size
grid(1..30).

% All cells that appear in the input
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one colour per cell (including 0)
{output(R,C,Col) : color(Col)} 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Preserve originally coloured cells (non‑zero)
:- input(R,C,In), In != 0, output(R,C,Out), Out != In.

% ----- Guess a solid rectangle of a non‑zero colour (pattern) -----
pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= 30, C2 <= 30,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% The pattern rectangle must be uniform of colour Col
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,Col).

% The pattern rectangle must not contain another colour
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col.

% ----- Guess a zero rectangle (destination) -----
zero(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= 30, C2 <= 30,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% The zero rectangle must be all zeros
:- zero(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,0).

% ----- Matching rectangles (same size) -----
matching(PR1,PR2,PC1,PC2,ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2,Col) :-
    pattern(PR1,PR2,PC1,PC2,Col),
    zero(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
    (PR2-PR1) = (ZR2-ZR1),
    (PC2-PC1) = (ZC2-ZC1).

% cells that receive the copied colour
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    matching(PR1,PR2,PC1,PC2,ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2,Col),
    R >= ZR1, R <= ZR2,
    C >= ZC1, C <= ZC2,
    % map (R,C) back to the source cell in the pattern
    PR = PR1 + (R - ZR1),
    PC = PC1 + (C - ZC1),
    input(PR,PC,Col).

% at least one copy must be performed
:- not copy_target(_,_,_).

% copy must be respected
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% zero cells that are not covered must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% copy must not overwrite a non‑zero cell
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0.
```

% copy cannot overwrite non-zero

Let's propose final program:

```
#const MAX=30.

% domain: rows and cols are from 1..MAX
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% exactly one output colour per cell (including 0)
{output(R,C,Col) : color(Col)} 1 :- cell(R,C).

% preserve originally coloured cells (non‑zero)
:- input(R,C,In), In != 0, output(R,C,Out), Out != In.

% ----- pattern rectangle (solid non‑zero colour) -----
pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% pattern rectangle must be uniform colour Col
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,Col).

% pattern rectangle must not contain another non‑zero colour
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col.

% ----- zero rectangle (destination) -----
zero(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% zero rectangle must be all zero
:- zero(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,0).

% matching rectangles (same size)
matching(PR1,PR2,PC1,PC2,ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2,Col) :-
    pattern(PR1,PR2,PC1,PC2,Col),
    zero(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
    (PR2-PR1) = (ZR2-ZR1),
    (PC2-PC1) = (ZC2-ZC1).

% cells that receive the copied colour
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    matching(PR1,PR2,PC1,PC2,ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2,Col),
    R >= ZR1, R <= ZR2,
    C >= ZC1, C <= ZC2,
    % map (R,C) back to source cell in the pattern
    SR = PR1 + (R - ZR1),
    SC = PC1 + (C - ZC1),
    input(SR,SC,Col).

% at least one copy must be performed
:- not copy_target(_,_,_).

% copy must be respected
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% zero cells not covered must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% copy must not overwrite a non‑zero cell
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0.
```

Define:

% Guess a zero rectangle (size >=2x2, top-left zero)
zero_rect(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% The rectangle must consist only of zeros
:- zero_rect(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,0).

% Guess a pattern rectangle (solid colour)
pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% The pattern rectangle must be uniform colour Col
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,Col).

% Pattern rectangle must not contain other colour
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col.

% Matching rectangles (same size)
matching(PR1,PR2,PC1,PC2,ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2,Col) :-
    pattern(PR1,PR2,PC1,PC2,Col),
    zero_rect(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
    (PR2-PR1) = (ZR2-ZR1),
    (PC2-PC1) = (ZC2-ZC1).

% copy_target cells
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    matching(PR1,PR2,PC1,PC2,ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2,Col),
    R >= ZR1, R <= ZR2,
    C >= ZC1, C <= ZC2,
    SR = PR1 + (R - ZR1),
    SC = PC1 + (C - ZC1),
    input(SR,SC,Col).

% at least one copy
:- not copy_target(_,_,_).

% copy must be respected
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% zero cells not covered must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% copy must not overwrite a non‑zero cell
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0.

% preserve originally coloured cells
:- input(R,C,In), In != 0, output(R,C,Out), Out != In.

% exactly one output colour per cell
{output(R,C,Col) : color(Col)} 1 :- cell(R,C).

% at least one zero rectangle (ensures we have a destination)
:- not zero_rect(_,_,_,_).
