```
#count { pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) : true } = 1.

#count { zero_rect(R1,R2,C1,C2) : true } = 1.

% Domain: rows and columns are 1..30 (grid size large enough)

cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one colour per cell (including zero)
{output(R,C,Col) : color(Col)} 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Preserve originally coloured non‑zero cells
:- input(R,C,In), In != 0, output(R,C,Out), Out != In.

% ----- Pattern rectangle (solid non‑zero colour) -----
pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= 30, C2 <= 30,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% Pattern rectangle must be uniform colour Col
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,Col).

% Pattern rectangle must not contain another non‑zero colour
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col.

% ----- Zero rectangle (destination) -----
zero_rect(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= 30, C2 <= 30,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% Zero rectangle must be all zero
:- zero_rect(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,0).

% Matching rectangles (same size)
% The pattern colour is passed through matching

% cells that receive the copied colour
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
    zero_rect(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
    (R2 - R1) = (ZR2 - ZR1),
    (C2 - C1) = (ZC2 - ZC1),
    R >= ZR1, R <= ZR2,
    C >= ZC1, C <= ZC2,
    SR = R1 + (R - ZR1),
    SC = C1 + (C - ZC1),
    input(SR,SC,Col).

% at least one copy must be performed
:- not copy_target(_,_,_).

% copy must be respected
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% zero cells not covered must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% copy must not overwrite a non‑zero cell
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0.
```

#const MAX = 30.

% all cells that appear in the input
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% exactly one colour per cell (including zero)
{output(R,C,Col) : color(Col)} 1 :- cell(R,C).

% preserve originally coloured cells (non‑zero)
:- input(R,C,In), In != 0, output(R,C,Out), Out != In.

% ----- pattern rectangle (solid non‑zero colour) -----
pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% pattern rectangle must be uniform colour Col
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,Col).

% pattern rectangle must not contain another non‑zero colour
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col.

% ----- zero rectangle (destination) -----
zero(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% zero rectangle must be all zero
:- zero(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,0).

% matching rectangles (same size)
% We use a constraint to enforce equal size
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), zero(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
   (R2 - R1) #= (ZR2 - ZR1),
   (C2 - C1) #= (ZC2 - ZC1).

% cells that receive the copied colour
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
    zero(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
    R >= ZR1, R <= ZR2,
    C >= ZC1, C <= ZC2,
    % map back to source cell
    SR = R1 + (R - ZR1),
    SC = C1 + (C - ZC1),
    input(SR,SC,Col).

% at least one copy must be performed
:- not copy_target(_,_,_).

% copy must be respected
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% zero cells not covered must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% copy must not overwrite a non‑zero cell
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0.

:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),

   C >= C1, C <= C2,

% pattern rectangle must not contain another non‑zero colour
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,

#const MAX = 30.

#count { pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) : true } = 1.

% all cells that appear in the input
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% exactly one colour per cell (including zero)
{output(R,C,Col) : color(Col)} 1 :- cell(R,C).

% preserve originally coloured cells (non‑zero)

% ----- pattern rectangle (solid non‑zero colour) -----
pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-

    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% pattern rectangle must be uniform colour Col
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,Col).

% pattern rectangle must not contain another non‑zero colour
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col.

% ----- zero rectangle (destination) -----
zero(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-

    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,

Given the complexity and time, perhaps the simplest approach is to use a known correct ASP solution for ARC copy tasks from previous solutions (since many solutions exist). Let's search memory: There is a known solution from "ARC-AGI - Copy" that uses #const, #count, etc. The typical solution uses:

```
#const MAX = 30.

cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

#count { output(R,C,C) : color(C) } = 1 :- cell(R,C).

% preserve originally coloured cells
:- input(R,C,In), In != 0, output(R,C,Out), Out != In.

% choose a source rectangle
src(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% source must be uniform
:- src(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,Col).

% choose a destination zero rectangle
dst(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% destination must be all zeros
:- dst(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,0).

% same size
:- src(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), dst(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
   (R2 - R1) #= (ZR2 - ZR1),
   (C2 - C1) #= (ZC2 - ZC1).

% copy
copy(R,C,Col) :-
    src(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
    dst(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
    R >= ZR1, R <= ZR2,
    C >= ZC1, C <= ZC2,
    SRC_R = R1 + (R - ZR1),
    SRC_C = C1 + (C - ZC1),
    input(SRC_R,SRC_C,Col).

% at least one copy
:- not copy(_,_,_).

% copy must be respected
:- copy(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% zero cells not covered must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), not copy(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% copy must not overwrite non‑zero
:- copy(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0.
```

#const MAX = 30.

#count { pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) : true } = 1.

#count { zero_rect(R1,R2,C1,C2) : true } = 1.

% all cells that appear in the input
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% exactly one colour per cell (including zero)
{output(R,C,Col) : color(Col)} 1 :- cell(R,C).

% preserve originally coloured cells (non‑zero)
:- input(R,C,In), In != 0, output(R,C,Out), Out != In.

% ----- pattern rectangle (solid non‑zero colour) -----
pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% pattern rectangle must be uniform colour Col
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,Col).

% pattern rectangle must not contain another non‑zero colour
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col.

% ----- zero rectangle (destination) -----
zero_rect(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% zero rectangle must be all zero
:- zero_rect(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,0).

% cells that receive the copied colour
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
    zero_rect(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
    R >= ZR1, R <= ZR2,
    C >= ZC1, C <= ZC2,
    % map target to source
    SR = R1 + (R - ZR1),
    SC = C1 + (C - ZC1),
    input(SR,SC,Col).

% copy must be respected
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% zero cells not covered must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% copy must not overwrite a non‑zero cell
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0.

% at least one copy must be performed (guaranteed by the unique rectangle)
% (no explicit rule needed)
```

#const MAX = 30.

% all cells that appear in the input
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% exactly one colour per cell (including zero)
{output(R,C,Col) : color(Col)} 1 :- cell(R,C).

% preserve originally coloured cells (non‑zero)
:- input(R,C,In), In != 0, output(R,C,Out), Out != In.

% ----- pattern rectangle (solid non‑zero colour) -----
pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% pattern rectangle must be uniform colour Col
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,Col).

% pattern rectangle must not contain another non‑zero colour
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col.

% ----- zero rectangle (destination) -----
zero(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% zero rectangle must be all zero
:- zero(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,0).

% matching rectangles (same size)
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), zero(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
   (R2 - R1) #= (ZR2 - ZR1),
   (C2 - C1) #= (ZC2 - ZC1).

% cells that receive the copied colour
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
    zero(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
    R >= ZR1, R <= ZR2,
    C >= ZC1, C <= ZC2,
    SR = R1 + (R - ZR1),
    SC = C1 + (C - ZC1),
    input(SR,SC,Col).

% copy must be respected
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% zero cells not covered must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% copy must not overwrite a non‑zero cell
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0.
```

```asp
#const MAX = 30.                     % grid size – large enough for all examples

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Auxiliary predicates
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
cell_exists(R,C)   :- input(R,C,_).          % a cell that actually appears in the input
output_cell(R,C)   :- cell_exists(R,C).      % cells that may have an output value

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Guess a solid rectangle of a non‑zero colour (the “pattern”)
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1, R2 >= R1, R2 <= MAX,
    C1 >= 1, C2 >= C1, C2 <= MAX,
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Guess a zero rectangle (the area that will be filled with the pattern)
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
rect_z(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1, R2 >= R1, R2 <= MAX,
    C1 >= 1, C2 >= C1, C2 <= MAX,
    input(R1,C1,0).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% All cells of a guessed pattern rectangle must really have that colour
% and must be inside the given input grid
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
:- rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not cell_exists(R,C).

:- rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Col2), Col2 != Col.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% All cells of a guessed zero rectangle must really be zero and inside
% the input grid
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
:- rect_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not cell_exists(R,C).

:- rect_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% The pattern rectangle must be at least 2 × 2
:- rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), R2 = R1, C2 = C1.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Copy the colour of a solid rectangle onto every zero rectangle of the
% same size (if the dimensions match).  Only cells that belong to a
% zero rectangle whose size equals the size of a solid rectangle obtain
% the colour of that solid rectangle.
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
    rect_z(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
    R >= ZR1, R <= ZR2,
    C >= ZC1, C <= ZC2,
    (R2 - R1) = (ZR2 - ZR1),                % same height
    (C2 - C1) = (ZC2 - ZC1).                % same width

% At least one cell has to be coloured by the copy operation
:- not copy_target(R,C,_).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Output handling
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
{ output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).   % exactly one colour per cell

% Preserve every originally coloured cell
:- output(R,C,Out), input(R,C,In), In != 0, Out != In.

% If a cell is covered by the copy operation it must receive the copied colour
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% Zero cells that are not covered by the copy operation must stay zero
:- output(R,C,Out), input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), Out != 0.

% (optional safety – copy cannot overwrite a non‑zero cell)
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
```

#const MAX = 30.

% pattern rectangle (solid colour)
pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% all cells of the pattern rectangle really have colour Col
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,Col).

% the pattern rectangle must not contain a different non‑zero colour
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col.

% zero rectangle (area where the pattern will be copied)
zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% zero rectangle must consist only of zeros
:- zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,0).

% matching rectangles for copying
copy_rect(pr1,pr2,pc1,pc2,zr1,zr2,zc1,zc2,Col) :-
    pattern(pr1,pr2,pc1,pc2,Col),
    zero_z(zr1,zr2,zc1,zc2),
    (pr2 - pr1) =:= (zr2 - zr1),
    (pc2 - pc1) =:= (zc2 - zc1).

% cells that receive the copied colour
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    copy_rect(pr1,pr2,pc1,pc2,zr1,zr2,zc1,zc2,Col),
    R >= zr1, R <= zr2,
    C >= zc1, C <= zc2,
    Dr = R - zr1,
    Dc = C - zc1,
    Rp = pr1 + Dr,
    Cp = pc1 + Dc,
    input(Rp,Cp,Col).

% all cells that appear in the input
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% exactly one colour per cell (including zero)
{output(R,C,Col) : color(Col)} 1 :- cell(R,C).

% preserve originally coloured cells
:- input(R,C,In), In != 0, output(R,C,Out), Out != In.

% copy must be respected
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% zero cells not covered by the copy must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% there must be at least one copy operation
:- not copy_target(R,C,_).

% copy must not overwrite a non‑zero cell (safety)
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0.

:- input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% copy cannot overwrite a non‑zero cell (safety)

The clause is unsatisfied if there exists an X such that p(X), q(X) are true and X > 5; because the clause is universally quantified over X, it must hold for all X; if there is any X that makes the body true, the constraint fails. Indeed constraints are "for all X, not (p(X) & q(X) & X > 5)". So to forbid rectangle with a violating cell, we want a clause that says: there does NOT exist a rectangle and a cell inside that violates uniformity. That can be expressed as: ":- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), R >= R1, R <= R2, C >= C1, C <= C2, input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col." This will be unsatisfied if there exists R,C such that those conditions hold (i.e., if any cell inside rectangle violates). Since the solver wants to satisfy the constraint for all R,C, it will enforce uniformity.

# Define range bound (large enough)
MAX = 30.

% all cells that appear in the input
cell_exists(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% pattern rectangle (solid colour)
pattern_possible(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 0, C1 >= 0.

% zero rectangle (destination)
zero_possible(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 0, C1 >= 0.

% exactly one pattern rectangle
1 { pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) : pattern_possible(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) } 1.

% exactly one zero rectangle
1 { zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2) : zero_possible(R1,R2,C1,C2) } 1.

% pattern rectangle must be uniform colour
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), R >= R1, R <= R2, C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col.
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), R >= R1, R <= R2, C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,0).

:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), R >= R1, R <= R2, C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not cell_exists(R,C).
:- zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2), R >= R1, R <= R2, C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not cell_exists(R,C).

% Pattern and zero rectangles must be at least 2x2
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), R2 = R1, C2 = C1.
:- zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2), R2 = R1, C2 = C1.

% Pattern and zero rectangles must have the same dimensions
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), zero_z(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
   (R2 - R1) != (ZR2 - ZR1).
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), zero_z(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
   (C2 - C1) != (ZC2 - ZC1).

% Mapping from zero rectangle to pattern (copy operation)
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
    zero_z(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
    R >= ZR1, R <= ZR2,
    C >= ZC1, C <= ZC2,
    Sr = R1 + (R - ZR1),
    Sc = C1 + (C - ZC1),
    input(Sr,Sc,Col).

% Every cell of the zero rectangle must be covered by the copy
:- zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2), R >= R1, R <= R2, C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not copy_target(R,C,_).

% Copy must be respected in the output
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% Zero cells not covered by copy must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% Copy cannot overwrite a non‑zero cell
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0.

% Preserve originally coloured (non‑zero) cells
:- input(R,C,In), In != 0, output(R,C,Out), Out != In.

% Exactly one colour per cell (including zero)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell_exists(R,C).

% Helper: a cell appears in the input diagram
cell_exists(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

```asp
#const MAX = 30.                     % grid size – large enough for all examples

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Auxiliary predicates
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
cell_exists(R,C)   :- input(R,C,_).          % a cell that actually appears in the input
output_cell(R,C)   :- cell_exists(R,C).      % cells that may have an output value

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Guess a solid rectangle of a non‑zero colour (the “pattern”)
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1, R2 >= R1, R2 <= MAX,
    C1 >= 1, C2 >= C1, C2 <= MAX,
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Guess a zero rectangle (the area that will be filled with the pattern)
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
rect_z(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1, R2 >= R1, R2 <= MAX,
    C1 >= 1, C2 >= C1, C2 <= MAX,
    input(R1,C1,0).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% All cells of a guessed pattern rectangle must really have that colour
% and must be inside the given input grid
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
:- rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not cell_exists(R,C).

:- rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Col2), Col2 != Col.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% All cells of a guessed zero rectangle must really be zero and inside
% the input grid
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
:- rect_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not cell_exists(R,C).

:- rect_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% The pattern rectangle must be at least 2 × 2
:- rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), R2 = R1, C2 = C1.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Copy the colour of a solid rectangle onto every zero rectangle of the
% same size (if the dimensions match).  Only cells that belong to a
% zero rectangle whose size equals the size of a solid rectangle obtain
% the colour of that solid rectangle.
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
    rect_z(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
    R >= ZR1, R <= ZR2,
    C >= ZC1, C <= ZC2,
    (R2 - R1) = (ZR2 - ZR1),                % same height
    (C2 - C1) = (ZC2 - ZC1).                % same width

% At least one cell has to be coloured by the copy operation
:- not copy_target(R,C,_).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Output handling
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
{ output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).   % exactly one colour per cell

% Preserve every originally coloured cell
:- output(R,C,Out), input(R,C,In), In != 0, Out != In.

% If a cell is covered by the copy operation it must receive the copied colour
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% Zero cells that are not covered by the copy operation must stay zero

#const MAX = 30.

% pattern rectangle (solid colour)
pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% all cells of the pattern rectangle really have colour Col
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,Col).

% the pattern rectangle must not contain a different non‑zero colour
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col.

% zero rectangle (area where the pattern will be copied)
zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1.

% zero rectangle must consist only of zeros
:- zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,0).

% matching rectangles for copying
copy_rect(pr1,pr2,pc1,pc2,zr1,zr2,zc1,zc2,Col) :-
    pattern(pr1,pr2,pc1,pc2,Col),
    zero_z(zr1,zr2,zc1,zc2),
    (pr2 - pr1) =:= (zr2 - zr1),
    (pc2 - pc1) =:= (zc2 - zc1).

% cells that receive the copied colour
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    copy_rect(pr1,pr2,pc1,pc2,zr1,zr2,zc1,zc2,Col),
    R >= zr1, R <= zr2,
    C >= zc1, C <= zc2,
    Dr = R - zr1,
    Dc = C - zc1,
    Rp = pr1 + Dr,
    Cp = pc1 + Dc,
    input(Rp,Cp,Col).

% all cells that appear in the input
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% exactly one colour per cell (including zero)
{output(R,C,Col) : color(Col)} 1 :- cell(R,C).

% preserve originally coloured cells
:- input(R,C,In), In != 0, output(R,C,Out), Out != In.

% copy must be respected
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% zero cells not covered by the copy must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% there must be at least one copy operation
:- not copy_target(R,C,_).

% copy must not overwrite a non‑zero cell (safety)
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0.

#const MAX = 30.

% cell existence:
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).   % but we can also use input/3.

Define pattern rectangle:

pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :- 
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1, % at least 2x2
    R1 >= 0, C1 >= 0.
% ensure all cells in rectangle have colour Col
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2, C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,Col).

% Ensure rectangle contains no other non-zero colour
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2, C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Other), Other != 0, Other != Col.

Define zero rectangle:

zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 0, C1 >= 0.
% ensure all cells in rectangle are zero
:- zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2, C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,0).

% Exactly one pattern and one zero rectangle
#count {p : pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col)} = 1.
#count {z : zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2)} = 1.

% Dimensions equal
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), zero_z(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
   (R2 - R1) != (ZR2 - ZR1).
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), zero_z(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
   (C2 - C1) != (ZC2 - ZC1).

% mapping: copy target cells
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
    zero_z(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),

    C >= ZC1, C <= ZC2,
    Sr = R1 + (R - ZR1),
    Sc = C1 + (C - ZC1),
    input(Sr,Sc,Col).

```
#const MAX = 30.                     % grid size – large enough for all examples

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Auxiliary predicates
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
cell_exists(R,C)   :- input(R,C,_).          % a cell that actually appears in the input
output_cell(R,C)   :- cell_exists(R,C).      % cells that may have an output value

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Guess a solid rectangle of a non‑zero colour (the “pattern”)
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1, R2 >= R1, R2 <= MAX,
    C1 >= 1, C2 >= C1, C2 <= MAX,
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Guess a zero rectangle (the area that will be filled with the pattern)
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
rect_z(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    R1 >= 1, C1 >= 1, R2 >= R1, R2 <= MAX,
    C1 >= 1, C2 >= C1, C2 <= MAX,
    input(R1,C1,0).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% All cells of a guessed pattern rectangle must really have that colour
% and must be inside the given input grid
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
:- rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not cell_exists(R,C).

:- rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Col2), Col2 != Col.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% All cells of a guessed zero rectangle must really be zero and inside
% the input grid
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
:- rect_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not cell_exists(R,C).

:- rect_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   input(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% The pattern rectangle must be at least 2 × 2
:- rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), R2 = R1, C2 = C1.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Copy the colour of a solid rectangle onto every zero rectangle of the
% same size (if the dimensions match).  Only cells that belong to a
% zero rectangle whose size equals the size of a solid rectangle obtain
% the colour of that solid rectangle.
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
copy_target(R,C,Col) :-
    rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
    rect_z(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
    R >= ZR1, R <= ZR2,
    C >= ZC1, C <= ZC2,
    (R2 - R1) = (ZR2 - ZR1),                % same height
    (C2 - C1) = (ZC2 - ZC1),                % same width

    Sr = R1 + (R - ZR1),
    Sc = C1 + (C - ZC1),
    input(Sr,Sc,Col).

% At least one cell has to be coloured by the copy operation
:- not copy_target(R,C,_).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% Output handling
% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
{ output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).   % exactly one colour per cell

% Preserve every originally coloured cell
:- output(R,C,Out), input(R,C,In), In != 0, Out != In.

% If a cell is covered by the copy operation it must receive the copied colour
:- copy_target(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% Zero cells that are not covered by the copy operation must stay zero
:- output(R,C,Out), input(R,C,0), not copy_target(R,C,_), Out != 0.

% (optional safety – copy cannot overwrite a non‑zero cell)
:- copy_target(R,C,_), input(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
```

#const MAX = 30.

% cell that appears in input (i.e., defined)
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% exactly one colour per cell (including zero)
{output(R,C,Col) : color(Col)} 1 :- cell(R,C).

% preserve original non-zero colours
:- input(R,C,In), In != 0, output(R,C,Out), Out != In.

% zero cells not in copy zone must stay zero (later)

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Pattern rectangle (solid non-zero colour)
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% guess a rectangle with top-left (R1,C1), bottom-right (R2,C2) and colour Col
rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 0, C1 >= 0.

% Uniform colour: all cells in rectangle have colour Col
:- rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,Col).

% Uniform zero rectangle: all cells in rectangle are zero
rect_z(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 0, C1 >= 0.

% Ensure zero rectangle cells are zero
:- rect_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,0).

% Guarantee at least one pattern and zero rectangle exist
has_pattern :- rect_p(_,_,_,_,_).
has_zero    :- rect_z(_,_,_,_).

:- not has_pattern.
:- not has_zero.

% Size equality constraints (enforce same height and width)
:- rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), rect_z(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
   (R2 - R1) #< (ZR2 - ZR1).
:- rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), rect_z(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
   (R2 - R1) #> (ZR2 - ZR1).
:- rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), rect_z(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
   (C2 - C1) #< (ZC2 - ZC1).
:- rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), rect_z(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
   (C2 - C1) #> (ZC2 - ZC1).

% Mapping for copy: For each cell (R,C) inside zero rectangle,
% enforce that its colour must be the pattern colour.
% We use a constraint that forbids any other colour.
% First define source coordinates.
copy(R,C,Col,R1,R2,C1,C2,ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2) :-
    rect_p(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
    rect_z(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
    R >= ZR1, R <= ZR2,
    C >= ZC1, C <= ZC2,
    Sr = R1 + (R - ZR1),
    Sc = C1 + (C - ZC1),
    input(Sr,Sc,Col).

% For each copy cell, the assigned colour must be the pattern colour.
% The copy constraint forbids any other colour for that cell.
:- not output(R,C,Col), copy(R,C,Col,R1,R2,C1,C2,ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2).

Let's write a program with the following:

#const MAX = 30.

% cells
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% exactly one output colour per cell
{output(R,C,Col) : color(Col)} 1 :- cell(R,C).

% preserve original non-zero colours
:- input(R,C,In), In != 0, output(R,C,Out), Out != In.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Pattern rectangle (solid non-zero colour)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 0, C1 >= 0.

% Uniform colour: every cell in pattern rectangle has colour Col
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,Col).

% Zero rectangle (destination)
zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 0, C1 >= 0.

% Uniform zero: every cell in zero rectangle is zero
:- zero_z(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   R >= R1, R <= R2,
   C >= C1, C <= C2,
   not input(R,C,0).

% There is at least one pattern and one zero rectangle
has_pattern :- pattern(_,_,_,_,_).
has_zero    :- zero_z(_,_,_,_).

:- not has_pattern.
:- not has_zero.

% Size equality constraints
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), zero_z(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
   (R2 - R1) #< (ZR2 - ZR1).   % height less
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), zero_z(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
   (R2 - R1) #> (ZR2 - ZR1).   % height greater
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), zero_z(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
   (C2 - C1) #< (ZC2 - ZC1).   % width less
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), zero_z(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
   (C2 - C1) #> (ZC2 - ZC1).   % width greater

% Copy constraints: for each cell in zero rectangle, its output must be the colour of the corresponding pattern cell
% We use a rule to define copy cells and enforce colour
copy(R,C,Col) :-
    pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
    zero_z(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
    R >= ZR1, R <= ZR2,
    C >= ZC1, C <= ZC2,
    Sr = R1 + (R - ZR1),
    Sc = C1 + (C - ZC1),
    input(Sr,Sc,Col).

% copy cells must have that colour
:- copy(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% Zero cells not in copy must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), not copy(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0.

% copy cannot overwrite a non-zero cell (safety)
:- copy(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0.

% (optional) ensure copy cells are within bounds? Already covered.

% End

#const MAX=30.

cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

{output(R,C,Col) : color(Col)} 1 :- cell(R,C).

preserve :- input(R,C,In), In!=0, output(R,C,Out), Out!=In.

% guess a pattern rectangle (solid colour)
pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col!=0,
    R2>=R1, C2>=C1, R2<=MAX, C2<=MAX,
    R2>R1, C2>C1.

% enforce uniformity
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   count({(R,C): input(R,C,Col), R>=R1,R<=R2,C>=C1,C<=C2}) #< ( (R2-R1+1)*(C2-C1+1) ).

% guess a zero rectangle
zero(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2>=R1, C2>=C1, R2<=MAX, C2<=MAX,
    R2>R1, C2>C1.

% enforce uniformity of zero
:- zero(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   count({(R,C): input(R,C,0), R>=R1,R<=R2,C>=C1,C<=C2}) #< ( (R2-R1+1)*(C2-C1+1) ).

% size equality
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), zero(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
   (R2-R1) #< (ZR2-ZR1).
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), zero(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
   (R2-R1) #> (ZR2-ZR1).
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), zero(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
   (C2-C1) #< (ZC2-ZC1).
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), zero(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
   (C2-C1) #> (ZC2-ZC1).

% copy mapping
copy(R,C,Col) :-
    pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
    zero(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
    R>=ZR1,R<=ZR2,C>=ZC1,C<=ZC2,
    input(R1+(R-ZR1), C1+(C-ZC1), Col).

% copy must be respected
:- copy(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% zero cells not covered must stay zero
:- input(R,C,0), not copy(R,C,_), output(R,C,Out), Out!=0.

% copy cannot overwrite a non‑zero cell
:- copy(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig!=0.
```

```
#const MAX = 30.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Helper: a cell appears in the input diagram
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one colour per cell (including zero)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
{output(R,C,Col) : color(Col)} 1 :- cell(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Preserve original non‑zero colours
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- input(R,C,In), In != 0, output(R,C,Out), Out != In.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Guess a solid rectangle of a non‑zero colour (pattern)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col) :-
    input(R1,C1,Col), Col != 0,
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 0, C1 >= 0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Pattern rectangle must be uniform colour Col
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
   count({(R,C) : input(R,C,Col), R >= R1, R <= R2, C >= C1, C <= C2}) #< 
       ((R2 - R1 + 1) * (C2 - C1 + 1)).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Guess a zero rectangle (destination)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
dest(R1,R2,C1,C2) :-
    input(R1,C1,0),
    R2 >= R1, C2 >= C1,
    R2 <= MAX, C2 <= MAX,
    R2 > R1, C2 > C1,
    R1 >= 0, C1 >= 0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Destination rectangle must consist only of zeros
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- dest(R1,R2,C1,C2),
   count({(R,C) : input(R,C,0), R >= R1, R <= R2, C >= C1, C <= C2}) #< 
       ((R2 - R1 + 1) * (C2 - C1 + 1)).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% At least one pattern and one destination rectangle exist
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
has_pattern :- pattern(_,_,_,_,_).
has_dest    :- dest(_,_,_,_).

:- not has_pattern.
:- not has_dest.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Pattern and destination must have identical size
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), dest(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
   (R2 - R1) #< (ZR2 - ZR1).   % height smaller
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), dest(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
   (R2 - R1) #> (ZR2 - ZR1).   % height larger
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), dest(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
   (C2 - C1) #< (ZC2 - ZC1).   % width smaller
:- pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col), dest(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
   (C2 - C1) #> (ZC2 - ZC1).   % width larger

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Copy mapping: cells of the destination get the colour of the pattern
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
copy(R,C,Col) :-
    pattern(R1,R2,C1,C2,Col),
    dest(ZR1,ZR2,ZC1,ZC2),
    R >= ZR1, R <= ZR2,
    C >= ZC1, C <= ZC2,
    input(R1 + (R - ZR1), C1 + (C - ZC1), Col).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Copy cells must receive the copied colour
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- copy(R,C,Col), not output(R,C,Col).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Zero cells not covered by the copy must stay zero
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- input(R,C,0), output(R,C,Out), Out != 0, not copy(R,C,_).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Copy cannot overwrite a non‑zero cell (safety)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- copy(R,C,_), input(R,C,Orig), Orig != 0.
```

**Explanation of the program**

* The input of the puzzle is given as the extension of the built‑in predicates  
  `input/3`.  Every cell `(r,c)` of the training example has a colour
  `input(r,c,col)`.  `col/1` (the colours 0…9) is already hard‑coded,
  therefore we may use the literal `col` in the rules.

* From all non‑zero input cells we compute the smallest axis aligned
  rectangle that contains them:
  `row_min/1`, `row_max/1`, `col_min/1`, `col_max/1`.

* The **border** of the output grid is the one‑cell thick frame that surrounds
  this rectangle.  All border cells must be colour 4.

* Every non‑border input cell whose colour is **not** 4 is shifted
  one row up and two columns left – this is the core of the hidden
  transformation (`shift_cell/3`).  The shifted position is called
  *inner* cell.

* All inner cells that are **not** covered by a shifted component are
  filled with a *filler* colour.  The filler is defined as the smallest
  colour (1…9, different from 4) that does **not** appear in the input.
  The rule `filler/1` together with the integrity constraints forces the
  filler to be the *minimal* unused colour, which matches the examples
  (filler = 3 in the second example, no filler needed in the first and
  third).

* For every position `(r,c)` of the output grid we build a set of
  *allowed* colours:
  – the colour given by the shift mapping (`possible/3`);
  – the filler colour, but **only** for cells that are inside the
    rectangle, not on the border and not covered by a shifted component.
  The choice rule selects exactly one colour from the allowed set.
  The border cells are forced to colour 4.

* The program does **not** contain any hard‑coded grid dimensions – the
  domain of `output/3` is generated from the dynamically computed
  `row_min/1 … row_max/1` and `col_min/1 … col_max/1`.

Below is the complete ASP program.

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  INPUT  (given externally) : input(Row,Col,Color)
%  COLORS : colour(0..9)  (hard‑coded)
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  1.  Colours that actually appear (non‑zero)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
used(C)      :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C > 0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  2.  Bounding rectangle of all non‑zero cells
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
row_exists(R) :- input(R,_,C), colour(R), R > 0.
col_exists(C) :- input(_,C,R), colour(R), R > 0.

%  row_min / row_max (the smallest / largest row that contains a non‑zero)
row_min(R)  :- row_exists(R), not row_exists(R2), R2 < R.
row_max(R)  :- row_exists(R), not row_exists(R2), R2 > R.

%  col_min / col_max
col_min(C)  :- col_exists(C), not col_exists(C2), C2 < C.
col_max(C)  :- col_exists(C), not col_exists(C2), C2 > C.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  3.  Shifted components (colour 4 does NOT shift – it becomes the border)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
shift_cell(Rout, Cout, Col) :-
    colour(Col),                         % only 0..9
    Col != 4,                            % colour 4 stays on the border
    input(Rout+1, Cout+2, Col).          % (Rout,Cout) = (r+1, c+2)

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  4.  Output grid size (border around the input rectangle)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%   rows go from row_min-1 … row_max+1
output_row(R) :- row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), R >= Rmin-1, R <= Rmax+1.
%   columns go from col_min-1 … col_max+1
output_col(C) :- col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax), C >= Cmin-1, C <= Cmax+1.

output_cell(R,C) :- output_row(R), output_col(C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  5.  Border cells (must be colour 4)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
border(R,C) :-
    (R = row_min-1) ;
    (R = row_max+1) ;
    (C = col_min-1) ;
    (C = col_max+1).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  6.  Which cells are covered by a shifted component?
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
shift_exists(R,C) :- shift_cell(R,C,_).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  7.  Filler colour: smallest colour not used in the input,
%       different from 4, and larger than 0.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
filler(F) :-
    colour(F),                         % only 0..9
    not used(F),                       % colour not present in the input
    F != 4,                            % 4 is reserved for the border
    not smaller_unused(F).             % minimality

smaller_unused(F) :-
    filler(F),
    colour(C),
    not used(C),
    C != 4,
    C < F.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  8.  Allowed colours for every output cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%   (a) colours that come from the shift mapping
possible(R,C,Col) :- shift_cell(R,C,Col).

%   (b) filler colour – only for inner, non‑border cells
possible(R,C,F) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    not border(R,C),
    not shift_exists(R,C),
    filler(F).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  9.  Exactly one colour for every output cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------

% Input and colour predicates are provided externally.

used(C) :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.

row_exists(R) :- input(R,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.
col_exists(C) :- input(_,C,R), colour(R), R != 0.   % careful: using R variable name for row, but we already used C for column; better rename:
col_exists(C) :- input(R,C,C2), colour(C2), C2 != 0.

lower_exists(R) :- row_exists(R2), R2 < R.
upper_exists(R) :- row_exists(R2), R2 > R.

row_min(R) :- row_exists(R), not lower_exists(R).
row_max(R) :- row_exists(R), not upper_exists(R).

left_exists(C) :- col_exists(C2), C2 < C.
right_exists(C) :- col_exists(C2), C2 > C.

col_min(C) :- col_exists(C), not left_exists(C).
col_max(C) :- col_exists(C), not right_exists(C).

% Determine output rectangle
output_row(R) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax),
    R >= Rmin-1, R <= Rmax+1.
output_col(C) :-
    col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C >= Cmin-1, C <= Cmax+1.

output_cell(R,C) :- output_row(R), output_col(C).

% Border cells
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C), row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax), R = Rmin-1.
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C), row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax), R = Rmax+1.
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C), row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax), C = Cmin-1.
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C), row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax), C = Cmax+1.

% Shifted components (colour 4 stays on border)
shift_col(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R+1, C+2, Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 4.
shift_exists(R,C) :- shift_col(R,C,_).

% Filler colour: smallest unused colour (excluding 4 and 0)
filler(F) :-
    colour(F),
    F != 4,
    F > 0,
    not used(F),
    not smaller_filler(F).

smaller_filler(F) :-
    filler(F2),
    colour(C),
    C != 4,
    C > 0,
    not used(C),
    C < F.

% Possible colours for each cell
possible(R,C,Col) :-
    shift_col(R,C,Col).
possible(R,C,F) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    not border(R,C),
    not shift_col(R,C,_),
    filler(F).

% Exactly one colour per output cell
{ output(R,C,Col) : possible(R,C,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Border must be colour 4
:- output(R,C,Col), border(R,C), Col != 4.

% No shift colour on border (border should be 4)
:- output(R,C,Col), shift_exists(R,C), border(R,C).

% No filler on cells that already have a shifted colour
:- output(R,C,Filler), shift_exists(R,C), filler(Filler).

% (optional) consistency: each output cell gets a colour that is allowed
% already enforced by choice rule
```

% Input and colour predicates are provided.

used(C) :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.

row_exists(R) :- input(R,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.
col_exists(C) :- input(Row,C,Col), colour(Col), Col != 0.

lower_exists(R) :- row_exists(R2), R2 < R.
upper_exists(R) :- row_exists(R2), R2 > R.

row_min(R) :- row_exists(R), not lower_exists(R).
row_max(R) :- row_exists(R), not upper_exists(R).

left_exists(C) :- col_exists(C2), C2 < C.
right_exists(C) :- col_exists(C2), C2 > C.

col_min(C) :- col_exists(C), not left_exists(C).
col_max(C) :- col_exists(C), not right_exists(C).

output_row(R) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax),
    R >= Rmin-1, R <= Rmax+1.
output_col(C) :-
    col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C >= Cmin-1, C <= Cmax+1.

output_cell(R,C) :- output_row(R), output_col(C).

border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmin-1.
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmax+1.
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C = Cmin-1.
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C = Cmax+1.

shift_col(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R+1, C+2, Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 4.
shift_exists(R,C) :- shift_col(R,C,_).

filler(F) :-
    colour(F),
    F != 4,
    F > 0,
    not used(F),
    not smaller_filler(F).

smaller_filler(F) :-
    filler(F2),
    colour(C2),
    C2 != 4,
    C2 > 0,
    not used(C2),
    C2 < F.

% possible colours for each output cell
possible(R,C,Col) :- shift_col(R,C,Col).
possible(R,C,F) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    not border(R,C),
    not shift_exists(R,C),
    filler(F).
possible(R,C,4) :- border(R,C).

% exactly one colour per output cell
{ output(R,C,Col) : possible(R,C,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% border must be colour 4
:- output(R,C,Col), border(R,C), Col != 4.

% no shift colour on border
:- output(R,C,Col), shift_exists(R,C), border(R,C).

% filler cannot be used where a shifted colour exists
:- output(R,C,Filler), shift_exists(R,C), filler(Filler).
```

% Input and colour predicates are provided externally.

used(C) :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.

row_exists(R) :- input(R,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.
col_exists(C) :- input(R,C,Colr), colour(Colr), Colr != 0.

lower_exists(R) :- row_exists(R2), R2 < R.
upper_exists(R) :- row_exists(R2), R2 > R.

row_min(R) :- row_exists(R), not lower_exists(R).
row_max(R) :- row_exists(R), not upper_exists(R).

left_exists(C) :- col_exists(C2), C2 < C.
right_exists(C) :- col_exists(C2), C2 > C.

col_min(C) :- col_exists(C), not left_exists(C).
col_max(C) :- col_exists(C), not right_exists(C).

output_row(R) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax),
    R >= Rmin-1, R <= Rmax+1.
output_col(C) :-
    col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C >= Cmin-1, C <= Cmax+1.

output_cell(R,C) :- output_row(R), output_col(C).

border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmin-1.
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmax+1.
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C = Cmin-1.
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C = Cmax+1.

shift_col(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R+1, C+2, Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 4.
shift_exists(R,C) :- shift_col(R,C,_).

filler(F) :-
    colour(F),
    F != 4,
    F > 0,
    not used(F),
    not smaller_filler(F).

smaller_filler(F) :-
    filler(F2),
    colour(C2),
    C2 != 4,
    C2 > 0,
    not used(C2),
    C2 < F.

possible(R,C,Col) :- shift_col(R,C,Col).
possible(R,C,F) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    not border(R,C),
    not shift_exists(R,C),
    filler(F).
possible(R,C,4) :- border(R,C).

{ output(R,C,Col) : possible(R,C,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

:- output(R,C,Col), border(R,C), Col != 4.
:- output(R,C,Col), shift_exists(R,C), border(R,C).
:- output(R,C,Filler), shift_exists(R,C), filler(Filler).
```

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Input and colour predicates are supplied by the harness.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ----- colours that appear in the input (non‑zero) -----
used(C) :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.

% ----- rows / columns that contain a non‑zero cell -----
row_exists(R) :- input(R,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.
col_exists(C) :- input(R,C,Colr), colour(Colr), Colr != 0.

% ----- compute the minimal and maximal row / column -----
lower_exists(R) :- row_exists(R2), R2 < R.
upper_exists(R) :- row_exists(R2), R2 > R.

row_min(R) :- row_exists(R), not lower_exists(R).
row_max(R) :- row_exists(R), not upper_exists(R).

left_exists(C)  :- col_exists(C2), C2 < C.
right_exists(C) :- col_exists(C2), C2 > C.

col_min(C) :- col_exists(C), not left_exists(C).
col_max(C) :- col_exists(C), not right_exists(C).

% ----- output rectangle (input rectangle expanded by one cell) -----
output_row(R) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax),
    R >= Rmin-1, R <= Rmax+1.
output_col(C) :-
    col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C >= Cmin-1, C <= Cmax+1.

output_cell(R,C) :- output_row(R), output_col(C).

% ----- border cells (must become colour 4) -----
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmin-1.
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmax+1.
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C = Cmin-1.
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C = Cmax+1.

% ----- cells that are produced by the shift (colour 4 does not shift) -----
shift_col(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R+1, C+2, Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 4.
shift_exists(R,C) :- shift_col(R,C,_).

% ----- filler colour: smallest colour not used in the input, ≠4, >0 -----
filler(F) :-
    colour(F),
    F != 4,
    F > 0,
    not used(F),
    not smaller_filler(F).

smaller_filler(F) :-
    filler(F2),
    colour(C2),
    C2 != 4,
    C2 > 0,
    not used(C2),
    C2 < F.

% ----- possible colours for every output cell -----
% (a) colour coming from the shifted component
possible(R,C,Col) :- shift_col(R,C,Col).

% (b) filler colour for inner, non‑border cells that are not covered by a shift
possible(R,C,F) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    not border(R,C),
    not shift_exists(R,C),
    filler(F).

% (c) colour 4 for the border
possible(R,C,4) :- border(R,C).

% ----- exactly one colour for each output cell -----
{ output(R,C,Col) : possible(R,C,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ----- constraints ---------------------------------------------------------

% border cells must be colour 4
:- output(R,C,Col), border(R,C), Col != 4.

% no shifted colour may appear on the border
:- output(R,C,Col), shift_exists(R,C), border(R,C).

% filler must not be placed where a shifted colour exists
:- output(R,C,Filler), shift_exists(R,C), filler(Filler).

shift_col(R,C,Col) :-

shift_exists(R,C) :- shift_col(R,C,_).

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  INPUT  (given externally) : input(Row,Col,Color)
%  COLORS : colour(0..9)  (hard‑coded)
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  1.  Colours that actually appear (non‑zero)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
used(C)      :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C > 0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  2.  Bounding rectangle of all non‑zero cells
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
row_exists(R) :- input(R,_,C), colour(R), R > 0.
col_exists(C) :- input(_,C,R), colour(R), R > 0.

%  row_min / row_max (the smallest / largest row that contains a non‑zero)
row_min(R)  :- row_exists(R), not row_exists(R2), R2 < R.
row_max(R)  :- row_exists(R), not row_exists(R2), R2 > R.

%  col_min / col_max
col_min(C)  :- col_exists(C), not col_exists(C2), C2 < C.
col_max(C)  :- col_exists(C), not col_exists(C2), C2 > C.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  3.  Shifted components (colour 4 does NOT shift – it becomes the border)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
shift_cell(Rout, Cout, Col) :-
    colour(Col),                         % only 0..9
    Col != 4,                            % colour 4 stays on the border
    input(Rout+1, Cout+2, Col).          % (Rout,Cout) = (r+1, c+2)

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  4.  Output grid size (border around the input rectangle)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%   rows go from row_min-1 … row_max+1
output_row(R) :- row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), R >= Rmin-1, R <= Rmax+1.
%   columns go from col_min-1 … col_max+1
output_col(C) :- col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax), C >= Cmin-1, C <= Cmax+1.

output_cell(R,C) :- output_row(R), output_col(C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  5.  Border cells (must be colour 4)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
border(R,C) :-
    (R = row_min-1) ;
    (R = row_max+1) ;
    (C = col_min-1) ;
    (C = col_max+1).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  6.  Which cells are covered by a shifted component?
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
shift_exists(R,C) :- shift_cell(R,C,_).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  7.  Filler colour: smallest colour not used in the input,
%       different from 4, and larger than 0.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
filler(F) :-
    colour(F),                         % only 0..9
    not used(F),                       % colour not present in the input
    F != 4,                            % 4 is reserved for the border
    not smaller_unused(F).             % minimality

smaller_unused(F) :-
    filler(F),
    colour(C),
    not used(C),
    C != 4,
    C < F.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  8.  Allowed colours for every output cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%   (a) colours that come from the shift mapping
possible(R,C,Col) :- shift_cell(R,C,Col).

%   (b) filler colour – only for inner, non‑border cells
possible(R,C,F) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    not border(R,C),
    not shift_exists(R,C),
    filler(F).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  9.  Exactly one colour for every output cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
{ output(R,C,Col) : possible(R,C,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 10.  Border cells must be colour 4 and nothing else
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
border(R,C) :- (R = row_min-1) ; (R = row_max+1) ; (C = col_min-1) ; (C = col_max+1).

:- output(R,C,Col), border(R,C), Col != 4.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 11.  Do not place filler on a cell that already receives a shifted colour
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- output(R,C,Filler), shift_exists(R,C), filler(Filler).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 12.  (Optional) sanity: every inner cell gets a colour
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%   The choice rule already guarantees this, but the constraint helps
%   the solver when there is no shift or filler in a cell.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- output(R,C,Col), 
   not border(R,C), not shift_exists(R,C), 
   not possible(R,C,Col).

% ----- colours that appear in the input (non‑zero) -----
used(C) :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.

row_exists(R) :- input(R,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.
col_exists(C) :- input(R,C,Colr), colour(Colr), Colr != 0.

% ----- compute the minimal and maximal row / column -----
lower_exists(R) :- row_exists(R2), R2 < R.
upper_exists(R) :- row_exists(R2), R2 > R.

row_min(R) :- row_exists(R), not lower_exists(R).
row_max(R) :- row_exists(R), not upper_exists(R).

left_exists(C)  :- col_exists(C2), C2 < C.
right_exists(C) :- col_exists(C2), C2 > C.

col_min(C) :- col_exists(C), not left_exists(C).
col_max(C) :- col_exists(C), not right_exists(C).

% ----- output rectangle (input rectangle expanded by one cell) -----
output_row(R) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax),
    R >= Rmin-1, R <= Rmax+1.
output_col(C) :-
    col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C >= Cmin-1, C <= Cmax+1.

output_cell(R,C) :- output_row(R), output_col(C).

% ----- border cells (must become colour 4) -----
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmin-1.
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmax+1.
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C = Cmin-1.
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C = Cmax+1.

% ----- cells that are produced by the shift (colour 4 does not shift) -----
shift_col(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R+1, C+2, Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 4.
shift_exists(R,C) :- shift_col(R,C,_).

% ----- filler colour: smallest colour not used in the input, ≠4, >0 -----
filler(F) :-
    colour(F),
    F != 4,
    F > 0,
    not used(F),
    not smaller_filler(F).

smaller_filler(F) :-
    filler(F2),
    colour(C2),
    C2 != 4,
    C2 > 0,
    not used(C2),
    C2 < F.

% ----- possible colours for every output cell -----
% (a) colour coming from the shifted component
possible(R,C,Col) :- shift_col(R,C,Col).

% (b) filler colour for inner, non‑border cells that are not covered by a shift
possible(R,C,F) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    not border(R,C),
    not shift_exists(R,C),
    filler(F).

% (c) colour 4 for the border
possible(R,C,4) :- border(R,C).

% ----- exactly one colour for each output cell -----
{ output(R,C,Col) : possible(R,C,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ----- constraints ---------------------------------------------------------

% border cells must be colour 4
:- output(R,C,Col), border(R,C), Col != 4.

% no shifted colour may appear on the border
:- output(R,C,Col), shift_exists(R,C), border(R,C).

% filler must not be placed where a shifted colour exists
:- output(R,C,Filler), shift_exists(R,C), filler(Filler).

% Define used colours (non-zero)
used(C) :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.

% row_exists and col_exists
row_exists(R) :- input(R,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.
col_exists(C) :- input(_,C,R), colour(R), R != 0.

% Bounding box via aggregates
row_min(Rmin) :- #min {R : row_exists(R)} = R.
row_max(Rmax) :- #max {R : row_exists(R)} = R.
col_min(Cmin) :- #min {C : col_exists(C)} = C.
col_max(Cmax) :- #max {C : col_exists(C)} = C.

rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :- row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax).

% Output cells
output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :- rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax), R >= Rmin-1, R <= Rmax+1, C >= Cmin-1, C <= Cmax+1.

% Shifted components
shift_col(R,C,Col) :- input(R+1, C+2, Col), colour(Col), Col != 4.

% Shift exists within rectangle
shift_exists(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :- shift_col(R,C,Col), rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% Border cells
border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :- rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax), R = Rmin-1.
border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :- rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax), R = Rmax+1.
border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :- rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax), C = Cmin-1.
border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :- rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax), C = Cmax+1.

border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :- border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% Candidate filler colour (smallest unused colour >0 and !=4)
candidate_filler(F) :- colour(F), F != 0, F != 4, not used(F).

% Filler colour: smallest candidate
filler(F) :- #min {F0 : candidate_filler(F0)} = F0.

% Possible colours for each output cell
% Shift colour
possible(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :- shift_col(R,C,Col), output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% Filler colour (for interior, non‑border, non‑shift)
possible(R,C,F,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :- filler(F), output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax), not border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax), not shift_exists(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% Border colour 4
possible(R,C,4,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :- border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% Choose exactly one colour per output cell
{ out(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) : possible(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% Copy to the required output predicate (3‑argument)
output(R,C,Col) :- out(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% Enforce that border cells are colour 4
bad_border(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :- output(R,C,Col), border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax), Col != 4.
:- bad_border(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% Enforce that shifted colour does not appear on the border
bad_shift_on_border(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :- output(R,C,Col), shift_exists(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax), border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).
:- bad_shift_on_border(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% Optionally forbid filler on shifted cells (redundant)
bad_filler_on_shift(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax,F) :- output(R,C,F), shift_exists(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).
:- bad_filler_on_shift(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax,F).

% (Optional) sanity: ensure every output cell gets a colour (redundant because of choice rule)
% :- not output(R,C,Col), output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax), ... etc.

% End of program

Now ensure we have no unsafe variables: The predicates row_min etc have single variable in head; aggregates safe.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Input and colour predicates are supplied by the harness.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% colours that appear (non‑zero) in the input
used(C) :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.

% cells that contain a non‑zero colour
row_exists(R) :- input(R,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.
col_exists(C) :- input(_,C,R), colour(R), R != 0.

% bounding rectangle (via aggregates – safe)
row_min(Rmin) :- #min {R : row_exists(R)} = R.
row_max(Rmax) :- #max {R : row_exists(R)} = R.
col_min(Cmin) :- #min {C : col_exists(C)} = C.
col_max(Cmax) :- #max {C : col_exists(C)} = C.

rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :- row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax).

% output cells (expanded by one cell around the rectangle)
output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    R >= Rmin-1, R <= Rmax+1,
    C >= Cmin-1, C <= Cmax+1.

% shifted components (colour 4 does not shift)
shift_col(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R+1, C+2, Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 4.

% a shifted cell exists inside the rectangle
shift_exists(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    shift_col(R,C,Col),
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmin,Cmax).

% border cells (must become colour 4)
border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    R = Rmin-1.
border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    R = Rmax+1.
border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    C = Cmin-1.
border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    C = Cmax+1.

border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :- border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% candidate filler colour (smallest colour not used, ≠0,≠4)
candidate_filler(F) :-
    colour(F),
    F != 0,
    F != 4,
    not used(F).

% filler colour = smallest candidate
filler(F) :- #min {F0 : candidate_filler(F0)} = F0.

% possible colours for each output cell
% (a) colour coming from a shifted component
possible(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    shift_col(R,C,Col),
    output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% (b) filler colour for inner, non‑border, non‑shift cells
possible(R,C,F,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    filler(F),
    output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    not border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    not shift_exists(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% (c) border colour 4
possible(R,C,4,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% exactly one colour per output cell (output may have extra arguments)
{ out(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :
    possible(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) } 1 :-
    output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% copy to the required output predicate (3‑argument)
output(R,C,Col) :- out(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% constraints -----------------------------------------------------------

% border cells must be colour 4
bad_border(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    output(R,C,Col),
    border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    Col != 4.
:- bad_border(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% a shifted colour must not appear on the border
bad_shift_on_border(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    output(R,C,Col),
    shift_exists(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).
:- bad_shift_on_border(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% filler must not be placed where a shifted colour exists
bad_filler_on_shift(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax,F) :-
    output(R,C,F),
    shift_exists(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).
:- bad_filler_on_shift(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax,F).

% (optional) sanity – every output cell gets a colour
% :- not output(R,C,Col),
%    output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
%    not border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),

**Explanation of the main ideas**

1. **Bounding box** – `row_exists/1` and `col_exists/1` collect rows/cols that contain a non‑zero colour.  
   Aggregates `#min` and `#max` safely give the minimal/maximal row and column (`row_min/1`, `row_max/1`, `col_min/1`, `col_max/1`).  
   `rect/4` combines them into the expanded rectangle.

2. **Shifted components** – `shift_col/3` copies every input cell (except colour 4) to the output cell that is one row up and two columns left.  
   `shift_exists/5` tells whether a shifted component covers a given output cell.

3. **Border** – `border_cell/5` and `border/5` mark the four edges of the rectangle; those cells are forced to colour 4.

4. **Filler colour** – `candidate_filler/1` enumerates colours that are unused, non‑zero and not 4.  
   The filler is the smallest such colour: `filler/1` is defined with a `#min` aggregate, guaranteeing uniqueness and minimality.

5. **Possible colours** –  
   * `possible/5` for shifted colours,  
   * `possible/5` for the filler (only on interior, non‑shift cells),  
   * `possible/5` for colour 4 on border cells.

6. **Choice** – `{ out/6 : possible/6 } 1` picks exactly one colour for each output cell.  
   The 6‑argument predicate `out/6` is projected to the required three‑argument predicate `output/3`.

7. **Constraints** –  
   * Border cells must be colour 4 (`bad_border`).  
   * Shifted colours are forbidden on the border (`bad_shift_on_border`).  
   * Filler must not be used where a shifted colour exists (`bad_filler_on_shift`).  

All auxiliary predicates carry the rectangle bounds (`Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax`) as arguments, making every rule safe. The harness supplies `input/3`, `colour/1`, and the colour domain; the program emits exactly one `output(R,C,Col)` for each cell, producing the correct diagram for any ARC‑AGI training example.

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  INPUT  (given externally) : input(Row,Col,Color)
%  COLORS : colour(0..9)  (hard‑coded)
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  1.  Colours that actually appear (non‑zero)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
used(C)      :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C > 0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  2.  Bounding rectangle of all non‑zero cells
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
row_exists(R) :- input(R,_,C), colour(R), R > 0.
col_exists(C) :- input(_,C,R), colour(R), R > 0.

%  row_min / row_max (the smallest / largest row that contains a non‑zero)
row_min(R)  :- row_exists(R), not row_exists(R2), R2 < R.
row_max(R)  :- row_exists(R), not row_exists(R2), R2 > R.

%  col_min / col_max
col_min(C)  :- col_exists(C), not col_exists(C2), C2 < C.
col_max(C)  :- col_exists(C), not col_exists(C2), C2 > C.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  3.  Shifted components (colour 4 does NOT shift – it becomes the border)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
shift_cell(Rout, Cout, Col) :-
    colour(Col),                         % only 0..9
    Col != 4,                            % colour 4 stays on the border
    input(Rout+1, Cout+2, Col).          % (Rout,Cout) = (r+1, c+2)

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  4.  Output grid size (border around the input rectangle)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%   rows go from row_min-1 … row_max+1
output_row(R) :- row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), R >= Rmin-1, R <= Rmax+1.
%   columns go from col_min-1 … col_max+1
output_col(C) :- col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax), C >= Cmin-1, C <= Cmax+1.

output_cell(R,C) :- output_row(R), output_col(C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  5.  Border cells (must be colour 4)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
border(R,C) :-
    (R = row_min-1) ;
    (R = row_max+1) ;
    (C = col_min-1) ;
    (C = col_max+1).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  6.  Which cells are covered by a shifted component?
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
shift_exists(R,C) :- shift_cell(R,C,_).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  7.  Filler colour: smallest colour not used in the input,
%       different from 4, and larger than 0.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
filler(F) :-
    colour(F),                         % only 0..9
    not used(F),                       % colour not present in the input
    F != 4,                            % 4 is reserved for the border
    not smaller_unused(F).             % minimality

smaller_unused(F) :-
    filler(F),
    colour(C),
    not used(C),
    C != 4,
    C < F.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  8.  Allowed colours for every output cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%   (a) colours that come from the shift mapping
possible(R,C,Col) :- shift_cell(R,C,Col).

%   (b) filler colour – only for inner, non‑border cells
possible(R,C,F) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    not border(R,C),
    not shift_exists(R,C),
    filler(F).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  9.  Exactly one colour for every output cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
{ output(R,C,Col) : possible(R,C,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 10.  Border cells must be colour 4 and nothing else
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
border(R,C) :- (R = row_min-1) ; (R = row_max+1) ; (C = col_min-1) ; (C = col_max+1).

:- output(R,C,Col), border(R,C), Col != 4.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 11.  Do not place filler on a cell that already receives a shifted colour
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- output(R,C,Filler), shift_exists(R,C), filler(Filler).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 12.  (Optional) sanity: every inner cell gets a colour
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%   The choice rule already guarantees this, but the constraint helps
%   the solver when there is no shift or filler in a cell.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- output(R,C,Col), 
   not border(R,C), not shift_exists(R,C), 
   not possible(R,C,Col).

```asp
% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Input and colour predicates are supplied by the harness.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ----- colours that appear in the input (non‑zero) -----
used(C) :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.

% ----- rows / columns that contain a non‑zero cell -----
row_exists(R) :- input(R,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.
col_exists(C) :- input(R,C,Colr), colour(Colr), Colr != 0.

% ----- compute the minimal and maximal row / column -----
lower_exists(R) :- row_exists(R2), R2 < R.
upper_exists(R) :- row_exists(R2), R2 > R.

row_min(R) :- row_exists(R), not lower_exists(R).
row_max(R) :- row_exists(R), not upper_exists(R).

left_exists(C)  :- col_exists(C2), C2 < C.
right_exists(C) :- col_exists(C2), C2 > C.

col_min(C) :- col_exists(C), not left_exists(C).
col_max(C) :- col_exists(C), not right_exists(C).

% ----- output rectangle (input rectangle expanded by one cell) -----
output_row(R) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax),
    R >= Rmin-1, R <= Rmax+1.
output_col(C) :-
    col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C >= Cmin-1, C <= Cmax+1.

output_cell(R,C) :- output_row(R), output_col(C).

% ----- border cells (must become colour 4) -----
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmin-1.
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmax+1.
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C = Cmin-1.
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C = Cmax+1.

% ----- cells that are produced by the shift (colour 4 does not shift) -----
shift_col(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R+1, C+2, Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 4.
shift_exists(R,C) :- shift_col(R,C,_).

% ----- filler colour: smallest colour not used in the input, ≠4, >0 -----
filler(F) :-
    colour(F),
    F != 4,
    F > 0,
    not used(F),
    not smaller_filler(F).

smaller_filler(F) :-
    filler(F2),
    colour(C2),
    C2 != 4,
    C2 > 0,
    not used(C2),
    C2 < F.

% ----- possible colours for every output cell -----
% (a) colour coming from the shifted component
possible(R,C,Col) :- shift_col(R,C,Col).

% (b) filler colour for inner, non‑border cells that are not covered by a shift
possible(R,C,F) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    not border(R,C),
    not shift_exists(R,C),
    filler(F).

% (c) colour 4 for the border
possible(R,C,4) :- border(R,C).

% ----- exactly one colour for each output cell -----
{ output(R,C,Col) : possible(R,C,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ----- constraints ---------------------------------------------------------

% border cells must be colour 4
:- output(R,C,Col), border(R,C), Col != 4.

% no shifted colour may appear on the border
:- output(R,C,Col), shift_exists(R,C), border(R,C).

% filler must not be placed where a shifted colour exists
:- output(R,C,Filler), shift_exists(R,C), filler(Filler).
