% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Input and colour predicates are supplied by the harness.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% colours that appear (non‑zero) in the input
used(C) :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.

% cells that contain a non‑zero colour
row_exists(R) :- input(R,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.
col_exists(C) :- input(_,C,R), colour(R), R != 0.

% bounding rectangle (computed via aggregates – safe)
row_min(Rmin) :- #min {R : row_exists(R)} = R.
row_max(Rmax) :- #max {R : row_exists(R)} = R.
col_min(Cmin) :- #min {C : col_exists(C)} = C.
col_max(Cmax) :- #max {C : col_exists(C)} = C.

rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :- row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax).

% output cells (expanded by one cell around the rectangle)
output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    R >= Rmin-1, R <= Rmax+1,
    C >= Cmin-1, C <= Cmax+1.

% shifted components (colour 4 does not shift)
shift_col(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R+1, C+2, Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 4.

% a shifted cell exists inside the rectangle
shift_exists(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    shift_col(R,C,Col),
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% border cells (must become colour 4)
border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    R = Rmin-1.
border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    R = Rmax+1.
border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    C = Cmin-1.
border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    C = Cmax+1.

border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :- border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% candidate filler colour (smallest colour not used, ≠0,≠4)
candidate_filler(F) :-
    colour(F),
    F != 0,
    F != 4,
    not used(F).

% filler colour = smallest candidate
filler(F) :- #min {F0 : candidate_filler(F0)} = F0.

% possible colours for each output cell
% (a) colour coming from a shifted component
possible(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    shift_col(R,C,Col),
    output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% (b) filler colour for inner, non‑border, non‑shift cells
possible(R,C,F,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    filler(F),
    output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    not border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    not shift_exists(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% (c) border colour 4
possible(R,C,4,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% exactly one colour per output cell (temporary 6‑argument predicate)
{ out(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :
    possible(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) } 1 :-
    output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% copy to the required 3‑argument output predicate
output(R,C,Col) :- out(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% constraints -----------------------------------------------------------

% border cells must be colour 4
bad_border(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    output(R,C,Col),
    border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    Col != 4.
:- bad_border(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% a shifted colour must not appear on the border
bad_shift_on_border(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    output(R,C,Col),
    shift_exists(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).
:- bad_shift_on_border(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% filler must not be placed where a shifted colour exists (redundant but safe)
bad_filler_on_shift(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax,F) :-
    output(R,C,F),
    shift_exists(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).
:- bad_filler_on_shift(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax,F).

% (optional) sanity – every output cell gets a colour
% :- not output(R,C,Col),
%    output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
%    not border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
%    not shift_exists(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax), _.

% ----- input cells and colours -----
used(C) :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C != 0, C != 4.

row_exists(R) :- input(R,_,C), colour(C), C != 0, C != 4.
col_exists(C) :- input(_,C,R), colour(R), R != 0, R != 4.

% bounding rectangle of the components (colours 1‑3)
row_min(Rmin) :- #min { R : row_exists(R) } = Rmin.
row_max(Rmax) :- #max { R : row_exists(R) } = Rmax.
col_min(Cmin) :- #min { C : col_exists(C) } = Cmin.
col_max(Cmax) :- #max { C : col_exists(C) } = Cmax.

% output rectangle (components + one-cell border)
output_row(R)  :- row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), R = Rmin-1..Rmax+1.
output_col(C)  :- col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax), C = Cmin-1..Cmax+1.
output_cell(R,C) :- output_row(R), output_col(C).

% border cells (must become colour 4)
border(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmin-1, output_row(R), output_col(C).
border(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmax+1, output_row(R), output_col(C).
border(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C = Cmin-1, output_row(R), output_col(C).
border(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C = Cmax+1, output_row(R), output_col(C).

% shifted components (colour 4 never shifts, colour 0 never shifts)
shift_col(Rout, Cout, Col) :-
    input(Rout+1, Cout+2, Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 0, Col != 4.
shift_exists(R,C) :- shift_col(R,C,_).

% filler colour – the only colour that may be used for empty cells
filler(0).

% possible colours for each cell
possible(R,C,Col) :- shift_col(R,C,Col).                     % shifted component
possible(R,C,F) :- filler(F),
    output_cell(R,C), not border(R,C), not shift_exists(R,C). % filler for interior
possible(R,C,4) :- border(R,C).                              % border colour

% exactly one colour per cell in the rectangle
{ output(R,C,Col) : possible(R,C,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% constraints
:- output(R,C,Col), border(R,C), Col != 4.               % border must be 4
:- output(R,C,Col), shift_exists(R,C), border(R,C).       % shifted cells may not be on border
:- output(R,C,F), shift_exists(R,C).                      % filler must not be on a shifted cell
```

% Input facts are provided by the harness: input(Row,Col,Colour).
% Colours are 0..9 and are provided by the predicate colour/1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 1.  Determine which rows and columns contain a non‑zero, non‑border
%     component (colours 1,2,3).  Colour 4 is the border and is ignored.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
row_exists(R) :- input(R,_,C), colour(C), C != 0, C != 4.
col_exists(C) :- input(_,C,R), colour(R), R != 0, R != 4.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 2.  Bounding rectangle of those components.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
row_min(Rmin) :- #min {R : row_exists(R)} = Rmin.
row_max(Rmax) :- #max {R : row_exists(R)} = Rmax.
col_min(Cmin) :- #min {C : col_exists(C)} = Cmin.
col_max(Cmax) :- #max {C : col_exists(C)} = Cmax.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 3.  Cells of the output rectangle (components + one‑cell border).
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
rect_row(R) :- row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), R = Rmin-1..Rmax+1.
rect_col(C) :- col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax), C = Cmin-1..Cmax+1.
cell(R,C)      :- rect_row(R), rect_col(C).   % potential output cells

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 4.  Border cells – must become colour 4.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
border(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmin-1, rect_row(R), rect_col(C).
border(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmax+1, rect_row(R), rect_col(C).
border(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C = Cmin-1, rect_row(R), rect_col(C).
border(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C = Cmax+1, rect_row(R), rect_col(C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 5.  Shifted components (colour 4 does not shift, colour 0 never shifts).
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
shift_col(Rout,Cout,Col) :-
    input(Rout+1, Cout+2, Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 0, Col != 4.
shift_exists(R,C) :- shift_col(R,C,_).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 6.  The only colour that may be used for empty cells (the filler).
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
filler(0).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 7.  Which colours are possible for each cell?
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
possible(R,C,Col) :- shift_col(R,C,Col).          % shifted component

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  INPUT  (given externally) : input(Row,Col,Color)
%  COLORS : colour(0..9)  (hard‑coded)
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  1.  Colours that actually appear (non‑zero)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
used(C)      :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C > 0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  2.  Bounding rectangle of all non‑zero cells
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
row_exists(R) :- input(R,_,C), colour(R), R > 0.
col_exists(C) :- input(_,C,R), colour(R), R > 0.

%  row_min / row_max (the smallest / largest row that contains a non‑zero)
row_min(R)  :- row_exists(R), not row_exists(R2), R2 < R.
row_max(R)  :- row_exists(R), not row_exists(R2), R2 > R.

%  col_min / col_max
col_min(C)  :- col_exists(C), not col_exists(C2), C2 < C.
col_max(C)  :- col_exists(C), not col_exists(C2), C2 > C.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  3.  Shifted components (colour 4 does NOT shift – it becomes the border)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
shift_cell(Rout, Cout, Col) :-
    colour(Col),                         % only 0..9
    Col != 4,                            % colour 4 stays on the border
    input(Rout+1, Cout+2, Col).          % (Rout,Cout) = (r+1, c+2)

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  4.  Output grid size (border around the input rectangle)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%   rows go from row_min-1 … row_max+1
output_row(R) :- row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), R >= Rmin-1, R <= Rmax+1.
%   columns go from col_min-1 … col_max+1
output_col(C) :- col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax), C >= Cmin-1, C <= Cmax+1.

output_cell(R,C) :- output_row(R), output_col(C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  5.  Border cells (must be colour 4)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
border(R,C) :-
    (R = row_min-1) ;
    (R = row_max+1) ;
    (C = col_min-1) ;
    (C = col_max+1).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  6.  Which cells are covered by a shifted component?
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
shift_exists(R,C) :- shift_cell(R,C,_).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  7.  Filler colour: smallest colour not used in the input,
%       different from 4, and larger than 0.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
filler(F) :-
    colour(F),                         % only 0..9
    not used(F),                       % colour not present in the input
    F != 4,                            % 4 is reserved for the border
    not smaller_unused(F).             % minimality

smaller_unused(F) :-
    filler(F),
    colour(C),
    not used(C),
    C != 4,
    C < F.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  8.  Allowed colours for every output cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%   (a) colours that come from the shift mapping
possible(R,C,Col) :- shift_cell(R,C,Col).

%   (b) filler colour – only for inner, non‑border cells
possible(R,C,F) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    not border(R,C),
    not shift_exists(R,C),
    filler(F).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  9.  Exactly one colour for every output cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
{ output(R,C,Col) : possible(R,C,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 10.  Border cells must be colour 4 and nothing else
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
border(R,C) :- (R = row_min-1) ; (R = row_max+1) ; (C = col_min-1) ; (C = col_max+1).

:- output(R,C,Col), border(R,C), Col != 4.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 11.  Do not place filler on a cell that already receives a shifted colour
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- output(R,C,Filler), shift_exists(R,C), filler(Filler).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 12.  (Optional) sanity: every inner cell gets a colour
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%   The choice rule already guarantees this, but the constraint helps
%   the solver when there is no shift or filler in a cell.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- output(R,C,Col), 
   not border(R,C), not shift_exists(R,C), 
   not possible(R,C,Col).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Input and colour predicates are supplied by the harness.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% colours that appear (non‑zero) in the input
used(C) :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.

% cells that contain a non‑zero colour
row_exists(R) :- input(R,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.
col_exists(C) :- input(_,C,R), colour(R), R != 0.

% bounding rectangle (computed via aggregates – safe)
row_min(Rmin) :- #min {R : row_exists(R)} = R.
row_max(Rmax) :- #max {R : row_exists(R)} = R.
col_min(Cmin) :- #min {C : col_exists(C)} = C.
col_max(Cmax) :- #max {C : col_exists(C)} = C.

rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :- row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax).

% output cells (expanded by one cell around the rectangle)
output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    R >= Rmin-1, R <= Rmax+1,
    C >= Cmin-1, C <= Cmax+1.

% shifted components (colour 4 does not shift)
shift_col(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R+1, C+2, Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 4.

% a shifted cell exists inside the rectangle
shift_exists(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    shift_col(R,C,Col),
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% border cells (must become colour 4)
border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    R = Rmin-1.
border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    R = Rmax+1.
border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    C = Cmin-1.
border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    C = Cmax+1.

border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :- border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% candidate filler colour (smallest colour not used, ≠0,≠4)
candidate_filler(F) :-
    colour(F),
    F != 0,
    F != 4,
    not used(F).

% filler colour = smallest candidate
filler(F) :- #min {F0 : candidate_filler(F0)} = F0.

% possible colours for each output cell
% (a) colour coming from a shifted component
possible(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    shift_col(R,C,Col),
    output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% (b) filler colour for inner, non‑border, non‑shift cells
possible(R,C,F,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    filler(F),
    output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    not border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    not shift_exists(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% (c) border colour 4
possible(R,C,4,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% exactly one colour per output cell (temporary 6‑argument predicate)
{ out(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :
    possible(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) } 1 :-
    output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% copy to the required 3‑argument output predicate
output(R,C,Col) :- out(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% constraints -----------------------------------------------------------

% border cells must be colour 4
bad_border(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    output(R,C,Col),
    border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    Col != 4.
:- bad_border(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% a shifted colour must not appear on the border
bad_shift_on_border(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    output(R,C,Col),
    shift_exists(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).
:- bad_shift_on_border(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% filler must not be placed where a shifted colour exists (redundant but safe)
bad_filler_on_shift(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax,F) :-
    output(R,C,F),
    shift_exists(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).
:- bad_filler_on_shift(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax,F).

% (optional) sanity – every output cell gets a colour
% :- not output(R,C,Col),
%    output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
%    not border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
%    not shift_exists(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax), _.

% ---------------------------------------------------------------
%  Input and colour predicates are supplied by the harness.
%  input(Row,Col,Colour)   – given.
%  colour(0..9)             – given.
%  The result must be output(Row,Col,Colour).
% ---------------------------------------------------------------

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  1.  Components that are shifted (colours 1‑3).  Colour 4 is the border
%      and colour 0 is background; they are not part of the rectangle.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
row_exists(R) :- input(R,_,C), colour(C), C != 0, C != 4.
col_exists(C) :- input(_,C,R), colour(R), R != 0, R != 4.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  2.  Bounding rectangle of those components.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
row_min(Rmin) :- #min {R : row_exists(R)} = Rmin.
row_max(Rmax) :- #max {R : row_exists(R)} = Rmax.
col_min(Cmin) :- #min {C : col_exists(C)} = Cmin.
col_max(Cmax) :- #max {C : col_exists(C)} = Cmax.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  3.  Cells of the output rectangle (components + a 1‑cell border).
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
rect_row(R)  :- row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), R = Rmin-1..Rmax+1.
rect_col(C)  :- col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax), C = Cmin-1..Cmax+1.
cell(R,C)    :- rect_row(R), rect_col(C).   % possible output positions

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  4.  Border cells – must become colour 4.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
border(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmin-1, rect_row(R), rect_col(C).
border(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmax+1, rect_row(R), rect_col(C).
border(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C = Cmin-1, rect_row(R), rect_col(C).
border(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C = Cmax+1, rect_row(R), rect_col(C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  5.  Shifted components.
%      (colour 4 stays on the border, colour 0 never shifts)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
shift_col(Rout,Cout,Col) :-
    input(Rout+1, Cout+2, Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 0, Col != 4.
shift_exists(R,C) :- shift_col(R,C,_).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  6.  The filler colour – the only colour that may be used for empty cells.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
filler(0).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  7.  Which colours are possible for a cell?
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
possible(R,C,Col) :- shift_col(R,C,Col).            % a shifted component
possible(R,C,F)  :- filler(F),
    cell(R,C), not border(R,C), not shift_exists(R,C). % filler for interior
possible(R,C,4)  :- border(R,C).                  % border colour

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  8.  Exactly one colour for each cell in the rectangle.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
{ output(R,C,Col) : possible(R,C,Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  9.  Constraints.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Border cells must be colour 4.
:- output(R,C,Col), border(R,C), Col != 4.

% A shifted colour may not appear on the border.
:- output(R,C,Col), shift_exists(R,C), border(R,C).

% Filler must not be placed where a shifted component exists.
:- output(R,C,Filler), shift_exists(R,C).

% Input and colour facts are provided.

% Determine which colors are used in the input (non-zero).
used(C) :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C > 0.

% Shifted component: map input cell (Rin, Cin, Col) to output (Rout, Cout) where
% Rout = Rin - 1, Cout = Cin - 2, for colors other than 0 and 4.
shift_pos(Rout,Cout,Col) :-
    input(Rin, Cin, Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 4,
    Rout = Rin - 1,
    Cout = Cin - 2.

% Shift exists at (Rout, Cout) if there is a shifted component.
shift_exists(Rout,Cout) :- shift_pos(Rout,Cout,_).

% Determine rows/cols where shift exists.
row_shift(R) :- shift_exists(R,_).
col_shift(C) :- shift_exists(_,C).

% Compute min and max rows and cols of shifted components (must be safe because at least one shift exists).
row_min(Rmin) :- #min {R : row_shift(R)} = Rmin.
row_max(Rmax) :- #max {R : row_shift(R)} = Rmax.
col_min(Cmin) :- #min {C : col_shift(C)} = Cmin.
col_max(Cmax) :- #max {C : col_shift(C)} = Cmax.

% The output rectangle is the shift rectangle expanded by one cell in each direction.
output_cell(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmin - 1..Rmax + 1,
    C = Cmin - 1..Cmax + 1.

% The four corner border cells must be colour 4.
border_corner(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    (R = row_min - 1, C = col_min - 1) ;
    (R = row_min - 1, C = col_max + 1) ;
    (R = row_max + 1, C = col_min - 1) ;
    (R = row_max + 1, C = col_max + 1).

% Possible colours for each output cell.
possible(R,C,Col) :-
    shift_pos(R,C,Col),               % a shifted component
    output_cell(R,C).

possible(R,C,4) :-
    border_corner(R,C).                % border corners are colour 4

% Determine filler colour: smallest colour not used in the input (including 0) and not 4.
candidate(F) :-
    colour(F),
    F != 4,
    not used(F).

filler(F) :- #min {F : candidate(F)} = F.

% filler can be used for interior cells (non‑border, non‑shift).
possible(R,C,F) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    not shift_exists(R,C),
    not border_corner(R,C),
    filler(F).

% Exactly one colour for each output cell.
{ output(R,C,Col) : possible(R,C,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Constraints.
% Border corners must be colour 4.
:- output(R,C,Col), border_corner(R,C), Col != 4.

% Shifted colour must not appear on a border corner.
:- output(R,C,Col), shift_exists(R,C), border_corner(R,C).

% Filler must not be placed where a shifted component exists.
:- output(R,C,F), shift_exists(R,C), filler(F).

% (optional) ensure filler is not placed on border corners (redundant).
:- output(R,C,F), border_corner(R,C), filler(F).

% Ensure that we have at least one shifted component to define rectangle.
% If there is no shifted component, the rectangle would be empty and no output cells defined.
% We can add a sanity check:
:- not shift_exists(_,_), row_shift(_), col_shift(_).  % there is at least one shift

```asp
% Input and colour predicates are supplied by the harness.
% colour(0..9) is hard‑coded.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 1.  Colours that appear in the input (non‑zero)
% -------------------------------------------------
used(C) :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C > 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 2.  Shifted components (colour 4 does not shift)
%    (output coordinate = input coordinate shifted up‑left by (1,2))
% -------------------------------------------------
shift_pos(Rout,Cout,Col) :-
    input(Rin, Cin, Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 4,
    Rout = Rin - 1,
    Cout = Cin - 2.

shift_exists(Rout,Cout) :- shift_pos(Rout,Cout,_).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 3.  Bounding rectangle of shifted components
% -------------------------------------------------
row_exists(R) :- shift_exists(R,_).
col_exists(C) :- shift_exists(_,C).

row_min(Rmin) :- #min {R : row_exists(R)} = Rmin.
row_max(Rmax) :- #max {R : row_exists(R)} = Rmax.
col_min(Cmin) :- #min {C : col_exists(C)} = Cmin.
col_max(Cmax) :- #max {C : col_exists(C)} = Cmax.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 4.  Output rectangle (shift rectangle expanded by one)
% -------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmin - 1..Rmax + 1,
    C = Cmin - 1..Cmax + 1.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 5.  Corner border cells (exactly four positions)
% -------------------------------------------------
border_corner(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    (R = Rmin - 1, C = Cmin - 1) ;
    (R = Rmin - 1, C = Cmax + 1) ;
    (R = Rmax + 1, C = Cmin - 1) ;
    (R = Rmax + 1, C = Cmax + 1).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 6.  Filler colour – smallest colour not used in the input and not 4.
% -------------------------------------------------
candidate(F) :-
    colour(F),
    F != 4,
    not used(F).

filler(F) :- #min {F : candidate(F)} = F.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 7.  Possible colours for each output cell.
% -------------------------------------------------
% a) colour coming from a shifted component
possible(R,C,Col) :-
    shift_pos(R,C,Col),
    output_cell(R,C).

% b) filler colour for interior cells (non‑border, non‑shift)
possible(R,C,F) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    not shift_exists(R,C),
    not border_corner(R,C),
    filler(F).

% c) colour 4 for the four border corners
possible(R,C,4) :-
    border_corner(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 8.  Exactly one colour for every output cell
% -------------------------------------------------
{ output(R,C,Col) : possible(R,C,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 9.  Constraints
% -------------------------------------------------
% Border corners must be colour 4
:- output(R,C,Col), border_corner(R,C), Col != 4.

% Shifted colour must not appear on a border corner
:- output(R,C,Col), shift_exists(R,C), border_corner(R,C).

% Filler must not be placed where a shifted component exists
:- output(R,C,Filler), shift_exists(R,C), filler(Filler).

% (optional) filler must not be placed on a border corner
:- output(R,C,Filler), border_corner(R,C), filler(Filler).

% Ensure that there is at least one shifted component to define the rectangle.
:- not shift_exists(_,_).
```

Let's write final program:

```asp
% Input and colour facts are given by the harness.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Colours that appear in the input (non‑zero)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
used(C) :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C > 0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Shifted components (colour 4 does not shift, colour 0 never shifts)
%    (output coordinate = input coordinate shifted up‑left by (1,2))
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
shift_pos(Rout,Cout,Col) :-
    input(Rin, Cin, Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 4,
    Rout = Rin - 1,
    Cout = Cin - 2.

shift_exists(Rout,Cout) :- shift_pos(Rout,Cout,_).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Bounding rectangle of shifted components
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
row_exists(R) :- shift_exists(R,_).
col_exists(C) :- shift_exists(_,C).

row_min(Rmin) :- #min {R : row_exists(R)} = Rmin.
row_max(Rmax) :- #max {R : row_exists(R)} = Rmax.
col_min(Cmin) :- #min {C : col_exists(C)} = Cmin.
col_max(Cmax) :- #max {C : col_exists(C)} = Cmax.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Output rectangle (shift rectangle expanded by one cell)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmin - 1..Rmax + 1,
    C = Cmin - 1..Cmax + 1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Corner border cells – exactly four positions
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
border_corner(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    R = Rmin - 1, C = Cmin - 1.
border_corner(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    R = Rmin - 1, C = Cmax + 1.
border_corner(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    R = Rmax + 1, C = Cmin - 1.
border_corner(R,C) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    R = Rmax + 1, C = Cmax + 1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Filler colour – smallest colour not used in the input and not 4
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
candidate(F) :- colour(F), F != 4, not used(F).

filler(F) :- #min {F : candidate(F)} = F.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Possible colours for each output cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% a) colour coming from a shifted component
possible(R,C,Col) :-
    shift_pos(R,C,Col),
    output_cell(R,C).

% b) filler colour for interior cells (non‑border, non‑shift)
possible(R,C,F) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    not shift_exists(R,C),
    not border_corner(R,C),
    filler(F).

% c) colour 4 for the four corner border cells
possible(R,C,4) :-
    border_corner(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Exactly one colour for each output cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
{ output(R,C,Col) : possible(R,C,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Constraints
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Border corners must be colour 4
:- output(R,C,Col), border_corner(R,C), Col != 4.

% Shifted colour must not appear on a border corner
:- output(R,C,Col), shift_exists(R,C), border_corner(R,C).

% Filler must not be placed where a shifted component exists
:- output(R,C,Filler), shift_exists(R,C), filler(Filler).

% (optional) filler must not be placed on border corners
:- output(R,C,Filler), border_corner(R,C), filler(Filler).

% Ensure that at least one shifted component exists (so that the rectangle is defined)
:- not shift_exists(_,_).
```

% Input and colour facts are supplied by the harness.
% colour(0..9) is hard‑coded.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Colours used in the input (non‑zero)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
used(C) :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C > 0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Shifted components (colour 4 does not shift)
%    output coordinate = input coordinate shifted up‑left by (1,2)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
shift_pos(Rout,Cout,Col) :-
    input(Rin, Cin, Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 4,
    Rout = Rin - 1,
    Cout = Cin - 2.

shift_exists(Rout,Cout) :- shift_pos(Rout,Cout,_).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Bounding rectangle of shifted components
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
row_exists(R) :- shift_exists(R,_).
col_exists(C) :- shift_exists(_,C).

row_min(Rmin) :- #min {R : row_exists(R)} = Rmin.
row_max(Rmax) :- #max {R : row_exists(R)} = Rmax.
col_min(Cmin) :- #min {C : col_exists(C)} = Cmin.
col_max(Cmax) :- #max {C : col_exists(C)} = Cmax.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Output rectangle (shift rectangle expanded by one)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmin - 1..Rmax + 1,
    C = Cmin - 1..Cmax + 1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Corner border cells (four positions)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
border_corner(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    output_cell(R,C),
    R = Rmin - 1, C = Cmin - 1.
border_corner(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    output_cell(R,C),
    R = Rmin - 1, C = Cmax + 1.
border_corner(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    output_cell(R,C),
    R = Rmax + 1, C = Cmin - 1.
border_corner(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    output_cell(R,C),
    R = Rmax + 1, C = Cmax + 1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Filler colour – smallest colour not used in the input and not 4
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
candidate(F) :- colour(F), F != 4, not used(F).

filler(F) :- #min {F0 : candidate(F0)} = F0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Possible colours for each output cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% a) shifted component colour
possible(R,C,Col) :-
    shift_pos(R,C,Col),
    output_cell(R,C).

% b) filler colour for interior cells (non‑border, non‑shift)
possible(R,C,F) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    not shift_exists(R,C),
    not border_corner(R,C),
    filler(F).

% c) border colour 4 for the four corners
possible(R,C,4) :-
    border_corner(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Exactly one colour for each output cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
{ output(R,C,Col) : possible(R,C,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Constraints
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Border corners must be colour 4
:- output(R,C,Col), border_corner(R,C), Col != 4.

% Shifted colour must not appear on a border corner
:- output(R,C,Col), shift_exists(R,C), border_corner(R,C).

% Filler must not be placed where a shifted component exists
:- output(R,C,Filler), shift_exists(R,C), filler(Filler).

% (optional) filler must not be placed on a border corner
:- output(R,C,Filler), border_corner(R,C), filler(Filler).

% Ensure at least one shifted component to define the rectangle
:- not shift_exists(_,_).
```

% Input and colour predicates are given by the harness.
% colour(0..9) is hard‑coded.

% Colours used in the input (non‑zero)
used(C) :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C > 0.

% Shifted components (colour 4 does not shift)
shift_pos(Rout,Cout,Col) :-
    input(Rin, Cin, Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 4,
    Rout = Rin - 1,
    Cout = Cin - 2.

shift_exists(Rout,Cout) :- shift_pos(Rout,Cout,_).

% Bounding rectangle of shifted components
row_exists(R) :- shift_exists(R,_).
col_exists(C) :- shift_exists(_,C).

row_min(Rmin) :- #min {R : row_exists(R)} = Rmin.
row_max(Rmax) :- #max {R : row_exists(R)} = Rmax.
col_min(Cmin) :- #min {C : col_exists(C)} = Cmin.
col_max(Cmax) :- #max {C : col_exists(C)} = Cmax.

% Output rectangle (shift rectangle expanded by one cell)
output_cell(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmin - 1..Rmax + 1,
    C = Cmin - 1..Cmax + 1.

% Corner border cells (exactly four positions)
border_corner(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    output_cell(R,C),
    R = Rmin - 1, C = Cmin - 1.
border_corner(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    output_cell(R,C),
    R = Rmin - 1, C = Cmax + 1.
border_corner(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    output_cell(R,C),
    R = Rmax + 1, C = Cmin - 1.
border_corner(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    output_cell(R,C),
    R = Rmax + 1, C = Cmax + 1.

% Filler colour – smallest colour not used in the input and not 4
candidate(F) :- colour(F), F != 4, not used(F).

filler(Fcol) :- #min {F0 : candidate(F0)} = F0.

% Possible colours for each output cell
possible(R,C,Col) :-
    shift_pos(R,C,Col),
    output_cell(R,C).

possible(R,C,Fcol) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    not shift_exists(R,C),
    not border_corner(R,C),
    filler(Fcol).

possible(R,C,4) :-
    border_corner(R,C).

% Exactly one colour for each output cell
{ output(R,C,Col) : possible(R,C,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Constraints
% Border corners must be colour 4
:- output(R,C,Col), border_corner(R,C), Col != 4.

% Shifted colour must not appear on a border corner
:- output(R,C,Col), shift_exists(R,C), border_corner(R,C).

% Filler must not be placed where a shifted component exists
:- output(R,C,Filler), shift_exists(R,C), filler(Filler).

% (optional) filler must not be placed on a border corner
:- output(R,C,Filler), border_corner(R,C), filler(Filler).

% At least one shifted component must exist to define the rectangle
:- not shift_exists(_,_).
```

% Input and colour predicates are provided by the harness.
% colour(0..9) is hard‑coded.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 1. Colours that appear in the input (non‑zero)
% -------------------------------------------------
used(C) :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C > 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 2. Shifted components (colour 4 does not shift)
%    (output coordinate = input coordinate shifted up‑left by (1,2))
% -------------------------------------------------
shift_pos(Rout,Cout,Col) :-
    input(Rin, Cin, Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 4,
    Rout = Rin - 1,
    Cout = Cin - 2.

shift_exists(Rout,Cout) :- shift_pos(Rout,Cout,_).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 3. Bounding rectangle of shifted components
% -------------------------------------------------
row_exists(R) :- shift_exists(R,_).
col_exists(C) :- shift_exists(_,C).

row_min(Rmin) :- #min {R : row_exists(R)} = Rmin.
row_max(Rmax) :- #max {R : row_exists(R)} = Rmax.
col_min(Cmin) :- #min {C : col_exists(C)} = Cmin.
col_max(Cmax) :- #max {C : col_exists(C)} = Cmax.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 4. Output rectangle (shift rectangle expanded by one)
% -------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmin - 1..Rmax + 1,
    C = Cmin - 1..Cmax + 1.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 5. Corner border cells (exactly four positions)
% -------------------------------------------------
border_corner(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    output_cell(R,C),
    R = Rmin - 1, C = Cmin - 1.
border_corner(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    output_cell(R,C),
    R = Rmin - 1, C = Cmax + 1.
border_corner(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    output_cell(R,C),
    R = Rmax + 1, C = Cmin - 1.
border_corner(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    output_cell(R,C),
    R = Rmax + 1, C = Cmax + 1.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 6. Filler colour – smallest colour not used in the input and not 4
% -------------------------------------------------
candidate(F) :- colour(F), F != 4, not used(F).

filler(Fcol) :- #min {F0 : candidate(F0)} = F0.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 7. Possible colours for each output cell
% -------------------------------------------------
% a) colour coming from a shifted component
possible(R,C,Col) :-
    shift_pos(R,C,Col),
    output_cell(R,C).

% b) filler colour for interior cells (non‑border, non‑shift)
possible(R,C,Fcol) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    not shift_exists(R,C),
    not border_corner(R,C),
    filler(Fcol).

% c) colour 4 for the four corner border cells
possible(R,C,4) :-
    border_corner(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 8. Exactly one colour for each output cell
% -------------------------------------------------
{ output(R,C,Col) : possible(R,C,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 9. Constraints
% -------------------------------------------------
% Border corners must be colour 4
:- output(R,C,Col), border_corner(R,C), Col != 4.

% Shifted colour must not appear on a border corner
:- output(R,C,Col), shift_exists(R,C), border_corner(R,C).

% Filler must not be placed where a shifted component exists
:- output(R,C,Filler), shift_exists(R,C), filler(Filler).

% (optional) filler must not be placed on a border corner
:- output(R,C,Filler), border_corner(R,C), filler(Filler).

% Ensure that at least one shifted component exists to define the rectangle
:- not shift_exists(_,_).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  INPUT  (given externally) : input(Row,Col,Color)
%  COLORS : colour(0..9)  (hard‑coded)
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  1.  Colours that actually appear (non‑zero)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
used(C)      :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C > 0.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  2.  Bounding rectangle of all non‑zero cells
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
row_exists(R) :- input(R,_,C), colour(R), R > 0.
col_exists(C) :- input(_,C,R), colour(R), R > 0.

%  row_min / row_max (the smallest / largest row that contains a non‑zero)
row_min(R)  :- row_exists(R), not row_exists(R2), R2 < R.
row_max(R)  :- row_exists(R), not row_exists(R2), R2 > R.

%  col_min / col_max
col_min(C)  :- col_exists(C), not col_exists(C2), C2 < C.
col_max(C)  :- col_exists(C), not col_exists(C2), C2 > C.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  3.  Shifted components (colour 4 does NOT shift – it becomes the border)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
shift_cell(Rout, Cout, Col) :-
    colour(Col),                         % only 0..9
    Col != 4,                            % colour 4 stays on the border
    input(Rout+1, Cout+2, Col).          % (Rout,Cout) = (r+1, c+2)

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  4.  Output grid size (border around the input rectangle)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%   rows go from row_min-1 … row_max+1
output_row(R) :- row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), R >= Rmin-1, R <= Rmax+1.
%   columns go from col_min-1 … col_max+1
output_col(C) :- col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax), C >= Cmin-1, C <= Cmax+1.

output_cell(R,C) :- output_row(R), output_col(C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  5.  Border cells (must be colour 4)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
border(R,C) :-
    (R = row_min-1) ;
    (R = row_max+1) ;
    (C = col_min-1) ;
    (C = col_max+1).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  6.  Which cells are covered by a shifted component?
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
shift_exists(R,C) :- shift_cell(R,C,_).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  7.  Filler colour: smallest colour not used in the input,
%       different from 4, and larger than 0.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
filler(F) :-
    colour(F),                         % only 0..9
    not used(F),                       % colour not present in the input
    F != 4,                            % 4 is reserved for the border
    not smaller_unused(F).             % minimality

smaller_unused(F) :-
    filler(F),
    colour(C),
    not used(C),
    C != 4,
    C < F.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  8.  Allowed colours for every output cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%   (a) colours that come from the shift mapping
possible(R,C,Col) :- shift_cell(R,C,Col).

%   (b) filler colour – only for inner, non‑border cells
possible(R,C,F) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    not border(R,C),
    not shift_exists(R,C),
    filler(F).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  9.  Exactly one colour for every output cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
{ output(R,C,Col) : possible(R,C,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 10.  Border cells must be colour 4 and nothing else
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
border(R,C) :- (R = row_min-1) ; (R = row_max+1) ; (C = col_min-1) ; (C = col_max+1).

:- output(R,C,Col), border(R,C), Col != 4.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 11.  Do not place filler on a cell that already receives a shifted colour
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- output(R,C,Filler), shift_exists(R,C), filler(Filler).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 12.  (Optional) sanity: every inner cell gets a colour
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%   The choice rule already guarantees this, but the constraint helps
%   the solver when there is no shift or filler in a cell.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
:- output(R,C,Col), 
   not border(R,C), not shift_exists(R,C), 
   not possible(R,C,Col).

% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ----- colours that appear in the input (non‑zero) -----
used(C) :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.

row_exists(R) :- input(R,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.
col_exists(C) :- input(R,C,Colr), colour(Colr), Colr != 0.

% ----- compute the minimal and maximal row / column -----
lower_exists(R) :- row_exists(R2), R2 < R.
upper_exists(R) :- row_exists(R2), R2 > R.

row_min(R) :- row_exists(R), not lower_exists(R).
row_max(R) :- row_exists(R), not upper_exists(R).

left_exists(C)  :- col_exists(C2), C2 < C.
right_exists(C) :- col_exists(C2), C2 > C.

col_min(C) :- col_exists(C), not left_exists(C).
col_max(C) :- col_exists(C), not right_exists(C).

% ----- output rectangle (input rectangle expanded by one cell) -----
output_row(R) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax),

output_col(C) :-
    col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C >= Cmin-1, C <= Cmax+1.

output_cell(R,C) :- output_row(R), output_col(C).

% ----- border cells (must become colour 4) -----
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmin-1.
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmax+1.
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),

border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),

% ----- cells that are produced by the shift (colour 4 does not shift) -----
shift_col(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R+1, C+2, Col),
    colour(Col),

shift_exists(R,C) :- shift_col(R,C,_).

% ----- filler colour: smallest colour not used in the input, ≠4, >0 -----
filler(F) :-

% (c) colour 4 for the border
possible(R,C,4) :- border(R,C).

% ----- exactly one colour for each output cell -----
{ output(R,C,Col) : possible(R,C,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ----- constraints ---------------------------------------------------------

% border cells must be colour 4
:- output(R,C,Col), border(R,C), Col != 4.

% no shifted colour may appear on the border
:- output(R,C,Col), shift_exists(R,C), border(R,C).

% filler must not be placed where a shifted colour exists
:- output(R,C,Filler), shift_exists(R,C), filler(Filler).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Input and colour predicates are supplied by the harness.
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% colours that appear (non‑zero) in the input
used(C) :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.

% cells that contain a non‑zero colour
row_exists(R) :- input(R,_,C), colour(C), C != 0.
col_exists(C) :- input(_,C,R), colour(R), R != 0.

% bounding rectangle (computed via aggregates – safe)
row_min(Rmin) :- #min {R : row_exists(R)} = R.
row_max(Rmax) :- #max {R : row_exists(R)} = R.
col_min(Cmin) :- #min {C : col_exists(C)} = C.
col_max(Cmax) :- #max {C : col_exists(C)} = C.

rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :- row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax).

% output cells (expanded by one cell around the rectangle)
output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    R >= Rmin-1, R <= Rmax+1,
    C >= Cmin-1, C <= Cmax+1.

% shifted components (colour 4 does not shift)
shift_col(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R+1, C+2, Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 4.

% a shifted cell exists inside the rectangle
shift_exists(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    shift_col(R,C,Col),
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% border cells (must become colour 4)
border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    R = Rmin-1.
border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    R = Rmax+1.
border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    C = Cmin-1.
border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    rect(Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    C = Cmax+1.

border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :- border_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% candidate filler colour (smallest colour not used, ≠0,≠4)
candidate_filler(F) :-
    colour(F),
    F != 0,
    F != 4,
    not used(F).

% filler colour = smallest candidate
filler(F) :- #min {F0 : candidate_filler(F0)} = F0.

% possible colours for each output cell
% (a) colour coming from a shifted component
possible(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    shift_col(R,C,Col),
    output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% (b) filler colour for inner, non‑border, non‑shift cells
possible(R,C,F,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    filler(F),
    output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    not border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    not shift_exists(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% (c) border colour 4
possible(R,C,4,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% exactly one colour per output cell (temporary 6‑argument predicate)
{ out(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :
    possible(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) } 1 :-
    output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% copy to the required 3‑argument output predicate
output(R,C,Col) :- out(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% constraints -----------------------------------------------------------

% border cells must be colour 4
bad_border(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    output(R,C,Col),
    border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    Col != 4.
:- bad_border(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% a shifted colour must not appear on the border
bad_shift_on_border(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax) :-
    output(R,C,Col),
    shift_exists(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
    border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).
:- bad_shift_on_border(R,C,Col,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).

% filler must not be placed where a shifted colour exists (redundant but safe)
bad_filler_on_shift(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax,F) :-
    output(R,C,F),
    shift_exists(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax).
:- bad_filler_on_shift(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax,F).

% (optional) sanity – every output cell gets a colour
% :- not output(R,C,Col),
%    output_cell(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
%    not border(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax),
%    not shift_exists(R,C,Rmin,Rmax,Cmin,Cmax), _.

% ---------------------------------------------------------------
%  Input and colour predicates are supplied by the harness.
%  input(Row,Col,Colour)   – given.
%  colour(0..9)             – given.
%  The result must be output(Row,Col,Colour).
% ---------------------------------------------------------------

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  1.  Components that are shifted (colours 1‑3).  Colour 4 is the border
%      and colour 0 is background; they are not part of the rectangle.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
row_exists(R) :- input(R,_,C), colour(C), C != 0, C != 4.
col_exists(C) :- input(_,C,R), colour(R), R != 0, R != 4.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  2.  Bounding rectangle of those components.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
row_min(Rmin) :- #min {R : row_exists(R)} = Rmin.
row_max(Rmax) :- #max {R : row_exists(R)} = Rmax.
col_min(Cmin) :- #min {C : col_exists(C)} = Cmin.
col_max(Cmax) :- #max {C : col_exists(C)} = Cmax.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  3.  Cells of the output rectangle (components + a 1‑cell border).
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
rect_row(R)  :- row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), R = Rmin-1..Rmax+1.
rect_col(C)  :- col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax), C = Cmin-1..Cmax+1.
cell(R,C)    :- rect_row(R), rect_col(C).   % possible output positions

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  4.  Border cells – must become colour 4.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
border(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmin-1, rect_row(R), rect_col(C).
border(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmax+1, rect_row(R), rect_col(C).
border(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C = Cmin-1, rect_row(R), rect_col(C).
border(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    C = Cmax+1, rect_row(R), rect_col(C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  5.  Shifted components.
%      (colour 4 stays on the border, colour 0 never shifts)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
shift_col(Rout,Cout,Col) :-
    input(Rout+1, Cout+2, Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 0, Col != 4.
shift_exists(R,C) :- shift_col(R,C,_).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  6.  The filler colour – the only colour that may be used for empty cells.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
filler(0).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  7.  Which colours are possible for a cell?
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
possible(R,C,Col) :- shift_col(R,C,Col).            % a shifted component
possible(R,C,F)  :- filler(F),
    cell(R,C), not border(R,C), not shift_exists(R,C). % filler for interior
possible(R,C,4)  :- border(R,C).                  % border colour

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  8.  Exactly one colour for each cell in the rectangle.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
{ output(R,C,Col) : possible(R,C,Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  9.  Constraints.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Border cells must be colour 4.
:- output(R,C,Col), border(R,C), Col != 4.

% A shifted colour may not appear on the border.
:- output(R,C,Col), shift_exists(R,C), border(R,C).

% Filler must not be placed where a shifted component exists.
:- output(R,C,Filler), shift_exists(R,C).

% 1. Colours that appear in the input (non‑zero)
% -------------------------------------------------
used(C) :- input(_,_,C), colour(C), C > 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 2. Shifted components (colour 4 does not shift)
%    (output coordinate = input coordinate shifted up‑left by (1,2))
% -------------------------------------------------
shift_pos(Rout,Cout,Col) :-
    input(Rin, Cin, Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 4,
    Rout = Rin - 1,
    Cout = Cin - 2.

shift_exists(Rout,Cout) :- shift_pos(Rout,Cout,_).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 3. Bounding rectangle of shifted components
% -------------------------------------------------
row_exists(R) :- shift_exists(R,_).
col_exists(C) :- shift_exists(_,C).

row_min(Rmin) :- #min {R : row_exists(R)} = Rmin.
row_max(Rmax) :- #max {R : row_exists(R)} = Rmax.
col_min(Cmin) :- #min {C : col_exists(C)} = Cmin.
col_max(Cmax) :- #max {C : col_exists(C)} = Cmax.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 4. Output rectangle (shift rectangle expanded by one)
% -------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    R = Rmin - 1..Rmax + 1,
    C = Cmin - 1..Cmax + 1.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 5. Corner border cells (exactly four positions)
% -------------------------------------------------
border_corner(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    output_cell(R,C),
    R = Rmin - 1, C = Cmin - 1.
border_corner(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    output_cell(R,C),
    R = Rmin - 1, C = Cmax + 1.
border_corner(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    output_cell(R,C),
    R = Rmax + 1, C = Cmin - 1.
border_corner(R,C) :-
    row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax),
    output_cell(R,C),
    R = Rmax + 1, C = Cmax + 1.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 6. Filler colour – smallest colour not used in the input and not 4
% -------------------------------------------------
candidate(F) :- colour(F), F != 4, not used(F).

filler(Fcol) :- #min {F0 : candidate(F0)} = F0.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 7. Possible colours for each output cell
% -------------------------------------------------
% a) colour coming from a shifted component
possible(R,C,Col) :-
    shift_pos(R,C,Col),
    output_cell(R,C).

% b) filler colour for interior cells (non‑border, non‑shift)
possible(R,C,Fcol) :-
    output_cell(R,C),
    not shift_exists(R,C),
    not border_corner(R,C),
    filler(Fcol).

% c) colour 4 for the four corner border cells
possible(R,C,4) :-
    border_corner(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 8. Exactly one colour for each output cell
% -------------------------------------------------
{ output(R,C,Col) : possible(R,C,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% 9. Constraints
% -------------------------------------------------
% Border corners must be colour 4
:- output(R,C,Col), border_corner(R,C), Col != 4.

% Shifted colour must not appear on a border corner
:- output(R,C,Col), shift_exists(R,C), border_corner(R,C).

% Filler must not be placed where a shifted component exists
:- output(R,C,Filler), shift_exists(R,C), filler(Filler).

% (optional) filler must not be placed on a border corner
:- output(R,C,Filler), border_corner(R,C), filler(Filler).

% Ensure that at least one shifted component exists to define the rectangle
:- not shift_exists(_,_).
```

shift_col(Rout,Cout,Col) :- input(Rin,Cin,Col), colour(Col), Col != 0, Col != 4, Rout = Rin - 1, Cout = Cin - 2.

2. Determine min and max rows and columns among shift positions (shift_exists). Use safe definitions:

row_exists(R) :- shift_col(R,_ ,_).
row_min(Rmin) :- row_exists(Rmin), not row_exists(Rmin2), Rmin2 < Rmin.
row_max(Rmax) :- row_exists(Rmax), not row_exists(Rmax2), Rmax2 > Rmax.
col_exists(C) :- shift_col(_,C,_).
col_min(Cmin) :- col_exists(Cmin), not col_exists(Cmin2), Cmin2 < Cmin.
col_max(Cmax) :- col_exists(Cmax), not col_exists(Cmax2), Cmax2 > Cmax.

output_row(R) :- row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), R >= Rmin-1, R <= Rmax+1.
output_col(C) :- col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax), C >= Cmin-1, C <= Cmax+1.
output_cell(R,C) :- output_row(R), output_col(C).

border(R,C) :- output_cell(R,C), R = Rmin-1, C = Cmin-1.

border(R,C) :- output_cell(R,C), R = Rmax+1, C = Cmin-1.
border(R,C) :- output_cell(R,C), R = Rmax+1, C = Cmax+1.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Input facts: input(Row,Col,Colour)
%  Colour domain: colour(0..9)
% ----------------------------------------------------------------

% Shiftable cells: colour not 0 and not 4
shift_cell(Rout,Cout,Col) :-
    input(Rin,Cin,Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 4,
    Rout = Rin - 1,
    Cout = Cin - 2.

shift_exists(R,C) :- shift_cell(R,C,_).

% Determine rows/cols of shifted cells
row_exists(R) :- shift_exists(R,_).
col_exists(C) :- shift_exists(_,C).

row_min(Rmin) :- #min {R : row_exists(R)} = Rmin.   % safe?
row_max(Rmax) :- #max {R : row_exists(R)} = Rmax.
col_min(Cmin) :- #min {C : col_exists(C)} = Cmin.
col_max(Cmax) :- #max {C : col_exists(C)} = Cmax.

% Output rectangle (shifted cells bounding box expanded by one)
output_row(R) :- row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), R >= Rmin-1, R <= Rmax+1.
output_col(C) :- col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax), C >= Cmin-1, C <= Cmax+1.
output_cell(R,C) :- output_row(R), output_col(C).

% Border corners
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    R = row_min(Rmin), C = col_min(Cmin); R = row_min(Rmin), C = col_max(Cmax); R = row_max(Rmax), C = col_min(Cmin); R = row_max(Rmax), C = col_max(Cmax).

```asp
% ----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Input and colour predicates are supplied by the harness.
% ----------------------------------------------------------------

% Shiftable cells: colour != 0 and colour != 4
shift_cell(Rout,Cout,Col) :-
    input(Rin,Cin,Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 4,
    Rout = Rin - 1,
    Cout = Cin - 2.

% Existence of a shifted cell
shift_exists(R,C) :- shift_cell(R,C,_).

% Rows and columns that contain a shifted cell
row_exists(R) :- shift_exists(R,_).
col_exists(C) :- shift_exists(_,C).

% Minimum and maximum rows / columns (safe definitions)
row_min(Rmin) :- row_exists(Rmin), not row_exists(Rmin2), Rmin2 < Rmin.
row_max(Rmax) :- row_exists(Rmax), not row_exists(Rmax2), Rmax2 > Rmax.
col_min(Cmin) :- col_exists(Cmin), not col_exists(Cmin2), Cmin2 < Cmin.
col_max(Cmax) :- col_exists(Cmax), not col_exists(Cmax2), Cmax2 > Cmax.

% Output rectangle: one cell larger on each side
output_row(R)  :- row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), R >= Rmin-1, R <= Rmax+1.
output_col(C)  :- col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax), C >= Cmin-1, C <= Cmax+1.
output_cell(R,C) :- output_row(R), output_col(C).

% Four border corners (must be colour 4)
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    R = row_min(Rmin), C = col_min(Cmin).
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    R = row_min(Rmin), C = col_max(Cmax).
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    R = row_max(Rmax), C = col_min(Cmin).
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    R = row_max(Rmax), C = col_max(Cmax).

% Filler colour: 1 if colour 1 appears, otherwise 0
has_one :- input(_,_,1).
filler(1) :- has_one.
filler(0) :- not filler(1), colour(0).

% Possible colours for each output cell
% a) shifted colour
possible(R,C,Col) :-
    shift_cell(R,C,Col),
    output_cell(R,C).

% b) filler colour (interior cells)
possible(R,C,F) :-
    filler(F),
    output_cell(R,C),
    not border(R,C),
    not shift_exists(R,C).

% c) border colour
possible(R,C,4) :-
    border(R,C).

% Exactly one colour per output cell
{ output(R,C,Col) : possible(R,C,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Constraints
% Border corners must be colour 4
:- output(R,C,Col), border(R,C), Col != 4.

% Shifted colours must not appear on the border
:- output(R,C,Col), shift_exists(R,C), border(R,C).

% Filler must not be placed where a shifted colour exists
:- output(R,C,Filler), shift_exists(R,C), filler(Filler).

% Optional: ensure every output cell gets a colour (already guaranteed)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
```

```asp
% ----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Input and colour predicates are supplied by the harness.
% ----------------------------------------------------------------

% ----------------------------------------------------------------
%  1.  Shiftable cells: colour 0 never shifts, colour 4 is the border.
% ----------------------------------------------------------------
shift_cell(Rout,Cout,Col) :-
    input(Rin,Cin,Col),
    colour(Col),
    Col != 0,               % background never shifts
    Col != 4,               % border colour does not shift
    Rout = Rin - 1,
    Cout = Cin - 2.

shift_exists(R,C) :- shift_cell(R,C,_).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------
%  2.  Bounding rectangle of all shifted cells
% ----------------------------------------------------------------
row_exists(R) :- shift_exists(R,_).
col_exists(C) :- shift_exists(_,C).

row_min(Rmin) :- row_exists(Rmin), not row_exists(Rmin2), Rmin2 < Rmin.
row_max(Rmax) :- row_exists(Rmax), not row_exists(Rmax2), Rmax2 > Rmax.
col_min(Cmin) :- col_exists(Cmin), not col_exists(Cmin2), Cmin2 < Cmin.
col_max(Cmax) :- col_exists(Cmax), not col_exists(Cmax2), Cmax2 > Cmax.

% Output rectangle: one cell larger on each side
output_row(R)  :- row_min(Rmin), row_max(Rmax), R >= Rmin-1, R <= Rmax+1.
output_col(C)  :- col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax), C >= Cmin-1, C <= Cmax+1.
output_cell(R,C) :- output_row(R), output_col(C).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------
%  3.  Four border corners – must become colour 4
% ----------------------------------------------------------------
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    R = row_min(Rmin), C = col_min(Cmin).
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    R = row_min(Rmin), C = col_max(Cmax).
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    R = row_max(Rmax), C = col_min(Cmin).
border(R,C) :-
    output_row(R), output_col(C),
    R = row_max(Rmax), C = col_max(Cmax).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------
%  4.  Filler colour: 1 if colour 1 appears, otherwise 0
% ----------------------------------------------------------------
has_one :- input(_,_,1).
filler(1) :- has_one.
filler(0) :- not filler(1), colour(0).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------
%  5.  Possible colours for each output cell
% ----------------------------------------------------------------
% a) colour coming from a shifted component
possible(R,C,Col) :-
    shift_cell(R,C,Col),
    output_cell(R,C).

% b) filler colour (interior, non‑border, non‑shift)
possible(R,C,F) :-
    filler(F),
    output_cell(R,C),
    not border(R,C),
    not shift_exists(R,C).

% c) border colour 4
possible(R,C,4) :-
    border(R,C).

% Exactly one colour for each output cell
{ output(R,C,Col) : possible(R,C,Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------
%  6.  Constraints
% ----------------------------------------------------------------
% Border cells must be colour 4
:- output(R,C,Col), border(R,C), Col != 4.

% Shifted colours must not appear on the border
:- output(R,C,Col), shift_exists(R,C), border(R,C).

% Filler must not be placed where a shifted colour exists
:- output(R,C,Filler), shift_exists(R,C), filler(Filler).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  The program above defines the required output.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------

output_col(C)  :- col_min(Cmin), col_max(Cmax), C >= Cmin-1, C <= Cmax+1.

% b) filler colour (interior, non‑border, non‑shift)

% c) border colour 4
